file.content:
- **Forcing an unauthed app state for mobile-mcp verification requires BOTH app-terminate AND keychain reset before relaunch.** `expo-secure-store` is iOS Keychain backed; `xcrun simctl keychain <udid> reset` only clears the keychain persistence layer, not the in-memory AuthProvider state of a running app. If the app is merely backgrounded, the `cachedToken` module-scope variable in `src/lib/storage.ts` + the React `user` state in `AuthProvider` both survive — and on foreground the AuthProvider does NOT re-read SecureStore (hydration runs once on mount). Correct sequence: `mobile_terminate_app(host.exp.Exponent)` → `xcrun simctl keychain <udid> reset` → `mobile_launch_app(host.exp.Exponent)` → tap the project in Expo Go's Recently Opened. The re-launch then mounts a fresh AuthProvider which hydrates from the (now empty) keychain and renders the role picker. Required for any story that verifies an auth-gated screen by logging out from outside the app (US-033 duplicate-username 409 test, and whenever US-037/US-038 UI lands before a sign-out-in-app flow exists).
- **mobile-mcp `type_keys` drops the first character or two of some inputs non-deterministically.** Observed on iPhone 17 Pro sim (iOS 26.3) with Expo Go — e.g. `u33_demo_17` → `u3_demo_17`, `dup-attempt@example.com` → `d-attempt@example.com`. If the typed value needs to match exactly (e.g. duplicate-conflict test), verify via `mobile_list_elements_on_screen` after each field and correct using `mobile_double_tap_on_screen` (triggers iOS word-selection menu) → tap "Select All" → retype. Don't rely on visual screenshot inspection to confirm input — the kerning hides a dropped char.
- **Defense-in-depth CHECK constraints mirror edge-function input regexes.** Edge functions that validate user-shaped strings (handles, usernames, slugs) via a `const RE = /^.../` before inserting are the first gate, but service_role writers (admin scripts, later migrations seeding fixtures, future edge functions reusing the same table) bypass that validation. Add a matching `CHECK (col::text ~ '<same-pattern>')` constraint on the column so the shape guarantee becomes a database invariant instead of a caller-by-caller convention. Before applying, run `select ... where col !~ '<pattern>'` and fix offenders — the ALTER will fail otherwise. Ref: `social_links_handle_format_chk` in `us_035_social_links_handle_check.sql` mirrors `HANDLE_RE` in `manage-social-link`/`auth-signup-creator`. Applies to any new user-string column (lister company slug, campaign slug, etc.).
- **RLS self-read policies must cover EVERY status the UI needs to surface, not just the happy path.** The `metric_snapshots_self_read` policy originally required `status='fresh'`, but us_011's hourly cron flips `is_latest` rows from `fresh` → `stale` in place — so the creator's "Stale" chip query returned zero rows (RLS silently filtered) even when the snapshot legitimately existed. Rule: audit every status enum on any RLS-gated table against the UI states that need to render it, and widen the policy accordingly. Keep `service_role`-only states (in-flight `refreshing`, error `failed`) off the self-read policy — those states are communicated to the client through edge-function responses, not direct table reads. Ref: `us_035_metric_snapshots_self_read_stale.sql`.
- **TabBar missing from a11y tree on some screens — use coord-clicks as the verified workaround.** `mobile_list_elements_on_screen` doesn't always report the custom `TabBar` (src/components/shared/TabBar.tsx) tab buttons inside nested route groups (observed on `/feed` during US-035 verification). The buttons are rendered and tappable, but absent from the accessibility tree snapshot. Workaround: take a screenshot to visually confirm the tab bar is present, then `mobile_click_on_screen_at_coordinates` at the known tab positions (on iPhone 17 Pro sim: ~y=820, x ≈ 44/130/217/303/352 for a 5-tab bar — inspect the screenshot for exact coords per layout). Don't block UI verification on missing a11y entries; confirm via screenshot + tap.
- **Supabase Realtime requires an explicit `supabase.realtime.setAuth(token)` before `channel().subscribe()` under the custom-JWT wrapper pattern.** `src/lib/supabase.ts` overrides `global.fetch` with `marketifyFetch` so HTTP requests carry our HS256 `MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET` token — but Realtime opens a separate websocket (`/realtime/v1`) that does NOT go through the fetch wrapper. Without `setAuth`, the websocket either auths as anon (no rows visible under RLS) or fails handshake entirely. Pattern: `await supabase.realtime.setAuth(getCachedToken()); channel = supabase.channel(...).on('postgres_changes', ...).subscribe();` then `removeChannel(channel)` in cleanup. Gate the whole block on a `cancelled` flag because setAuth is async and the effect may re-run or unmount before it resolves. Ref: `app/(creator)/profile.tsx` US-036 realtime effect.
- **Synchronous `useRef` lock for rapid-fire gesture handlers where React state deps would stale-capture.** When a `useCallback` has `[stateFlag, ...]` in its deps and is invoked by a gesture/press that can fire multiple times in the same render tick, the closure captures the PREVIOUS `stateFlag` — two pulls in a row both see `false` and both run. Pattern: pair a `useRef(false)` that the callback reads and writes synchronously (`if (lock.current) return; lock.current = true; ... finally { lock.current = false }`) with the React state that drives UI. Remove the state flag from the callback's deps since the ref is the actual guard. Same stale-capture mechanism as Pattern #82's Reanimated worklets applies to rapid-fire JS-side handlers (pull-to-refresh, double-press submits, scroll-triggered loads). Ref: `app/(creator)/profile.tsx` `refreshLock` in US-036.
- **Pan gesture coexisting with a parent ScrollView: use `activeOffsetY([15, 9999])` + `failOffsetX([-20, 20])` instead of `activateAfterLongPress`.** Long-press activation is unreliable when the user starts dragging quickly — ScrollView's native gesture wins and the Pan never fires. The threshold-based activation per RNGH canonical docs declares "only downward pulls past 15pt count as pan" without stealing all touches: upward scrolls and horizontal drifts are rejected, so the ScrollView handles normal content scroll, and a deliberate downward pull triggers the Pan. `failOffsetX` prevents a diagonal swipe from being misread as a pull. Inside `.onUpdate`, apply a piecewise-linear damping past threshold (`raw <= THRESHOLD ? raw : THRESHOLD + (raw - THRESHOLD) * 0.35`) for the rubber-band feel. Ref: `RefreshablePlatformCard` in `app/(creator)/profile.tsx`.
- **Supabase MCP `deploy_edge_function` bundles files exactly as you name them.** The entrypoint MUST be named `source/<entrypoint>.ts` (not `index.ts`) and the `entrypoint_path` field must match (`source/index.ts`). Shared modules live at bundle root as `_shared/<name>.ts` — NOT `source/_shared/<name>.ts`. The entrypoint imports them as `../_shared/<name>.ts` because the bundler resolves relative to `source/`. Mismatched layouts produce "Module not found `_shared/<name>.ts`" at deploy time with no other hint. Canonical reference: any sibling under `supabase/functions/*/index.ts` paired with the deploy `files: [{name: "source/index.ts", ...}, {name: "_shared/jwt.ts", ...}, ...]` — `metrics-refresh` is the earliest example, `get-listing-detail` follows the same layout.
- **When a story's AC conflicts with `docs/tech-architecture.md`, the spec wins — but additive fields are defensible when the spec is under-specified.** Ralph rule #1 is "specs win," and that holds for response envelopes, DB column shape, and anything the spec is explicit about. But specs often leave ambiguity at the field level — e.g. §5.4's `failed_conditions: Array<{ metric; required; actual }>` elides `platform` while `min_followers` is ambiguous across TikTok/Instagram. Pattern: align the envelope shape to the spec, but enrich with additive fields when the UI can't disambiguate from spec alone. Document the addition in the edge function's header comment block so future iterations see the rationale. Ref: `get-listing-detail/index.ts` returning `failed_conditions: [{metric, platform, required, actual}]` as a documented extension.
- **Concurrency via version-id pin + FOR UPDATE re-read, not engine duplication in PL/pgSQL.** When an edge function needs to "re-evaluate eligibility against the NOW-current listing version" (spec §5.5) without duplicating the TS eligibility engine in SQL, the sanctioned split is: (1) edge fn reads `listings.current_version_id` and runs TS eligibility against it, then (2) calls an RPC that re-reads the same listing under `select ... for update`, compares the locked `current_version_id` to `p_expected_version_id`, and returns `LISTING_VERSION_CHANGED` on mismatch so the client re-opens the detail view. The row lock serializes this flow against `app_private.bump_listing_version` (which runs under its own row lock on the listings UPDATE). Invariant depends on the bump trigger producing a new `current_version_id` for every eligibility-affecting mutation — if a future trigger change lets a condition edit land without bumping, the RPC would silently commit against stale TS eligibility. Document the dependency in the RPC header. Ref: `supabase/migrations/20260417270000_us_041_apply_to_listing_rpc.sql` + `apply-to-listing/index.ts`.
- **Fail-closed applies to malformed data, not just missing data.** Spec §3d's fail-closed rule covers the "null actual metric" case (user hasn't been scraped), but the same principle extends to unreadable THRESHOLD values: a `gte` pre-condition with a NULL or non-numeric `numeric_threshold` must fail the check, not silently pass. Silently returning `null` from the single-condition evaluator for a malformed row is how a future mis-migrated condition flips a listing to "always eligible" without any alarm. Rule: every branch of an eligibility-or-permission evaluator that hits unreadable input returns a fail, never an early "skip." Schema CHECKs should prevent the malformed row from existing, but the evaluator is the last line of defense. Ref: `_shared/eligibility.ts` `evaluatePreCondition`.
- **Extract shared modules under `supabase/functions/_shared/` as soon as a second function needs the same logic.** US-039's eligibility engine was inline; US-041 needed the same engine for its server-side re-check. Pattern: extract to `_shared/<name>.ts` with a header comment naming the consumers and the spec sections it implements, have BOTH functions import it via `../_shared/<name>.ts`, and **redeploy every consumer function** in the same iteration — the deployed bundle of a prior function still contains the inlined copy, so a future redeploy of that function without the shared file would fail with `Module not found`. The `deploy_edge_function` `files` array must include every `_shared/*.ts` dependency, not just the new one. Ref: `_shared/eligibility.ts` shared by `get-listing-detail` (v4) + `apply-to-listing` (v2).
- **`KeyboardAvoidingView` is unreliable inside an RN `<Modal>` (and therefore inside our `BottomSheet`).** The Modal opens its own native window, so KAV's measured frame and the keyboard's reported frame disagree and the contents do not get pushed up. Replace KAV with a direct `Keyboard.addListener('keyboardWillShow' / 'keyboardWillHide')` (use `keyboardDidShow`/`keyboardDidHide` on Android) that drives `paddingBottom` on the inner `<View>` from `e.endCoordinates.height`. Remember that `BottomSheet` snap point must accommodate `keyboard_height (~336px iOS) + content_height` — for the apply sheet that meant raising `snapPoints` from `[0.7]` to `[0.85]`. Ref: `src/screens/listing-detail/ApplySheet.tsx`.
- **Defer `router.replace`/`router.push` after dismissing a `BottomSheet` by ~350ms** so the sheet's spring-out animation (`SPRING_SNAPPY` settles in ~300ms — see `src/design/motion.ts`) plays cleanly before the new screen mounts. Without the delay, the sheet slides down on top of the freshly-rendered destination, which looks broken. The same delay applies to any imperative navigation triggered from a Modal/BottomSheet success path. Ref: `app/(creator)/listing/[id].tsx` `onApplied`.
- **Run cheap checks before upstream oEmbed/scraper calls in edge functions; return `502 OEMBED_UNAVAILABLE` (not 500) when the upstream itself fails.** Order inside an endpoint that relies on a third-party URL validator: JWT → role → parse body → regex-classify URL → DB lookups → status/permission gates → affirmation/eligibility check → upstream oEmbed → RPC. This is both a cost optimization (don't burn TikTok rate-limit slots on requests that will 4xx regardless) and a DDoS-amplifier guard (an unauth POST must not be able to force an upstream fetch). Split the error codes: `500 SERVER_MISCONFIGURED` / `500 DB_ERROR` for our-fault failures, `502 OEMBED_UNAVAILABLE` for upstream unavailability — clients that auto-retry benefit because upstream outages are usually retryable, DB bugs rarely are. Document BOTH codes in the contract comment. Ref: `supabase/functions/submit-video/index.ts` oEmbed section + error envelope header.
- **Defensive null-guards on NOT NULL columns fetched via service-role client, when the row feeds a security-relevant gate.** The database schema can enforce NOT NULL, but a TypeScript read from a future mis-migrated row that returns `null` will silently propagate into downstream logic. When the value gates a security check (e.g. `application.listing_version_id` → the post-condition affirmation check; an empty `.eq('listing_version_id', null)` fetch returns zero rows → no affirmations required → gate bypassed), add an explicit `if (!row.field) { 500 DB_ERROR }`. One extra branch is cheaper than a future exploitable silent-pass. Ref: `supabase/functions/submit-video/index.ts` listing_version_id guard.
- **PostgREST embeds silently NULL joined columns when the target table's RLS filters the caller out — always audit every `.select('..., related!fk(col)')` embed against the related table's RLS policies.** Failure mode: the embed syntax succeeds, returns rows, and populates the parent columns; the embedded field just comes back as `null` or `[]` under RLS because PostgREST applies the policies per-row transparently. Highest-risk embeds on this project: (a) `users!lister_id(username)` where `users_self_select` is self-only — every cross-tenant handle read (creator viewing lister handle, lister viewing applicant handle) will null out; (b) `listing_versions!...(...)` where `listing_versions_read_if_listing_readable` gates on `listings.status='active' OR caller=lister` — any historical/closed listing version the caller doesn't own will null; (c) `listings!...(title)` where `listings_public_read` requires `status='active'` — same closed-listing nullout. Rule: before writing a PostgREST embed, check the target table's RLS; if the policy wouldn't return the specific row under the caller's role, replace the embed with a SECURITY DEFINER RPC that does its own ownership check. DO NOT widen the self-only policy to "return usernames if handle is public" — that leaks the full row shape; DEFINER + narrow column projection is the correct primitive. Ref: Codebase Pattern #40 (`submission_reuse_count`), Codebase Pattern #110 (`apply_to_listing_rpc`), and `public.list_my_applications()` from US-043.
- **SECURITY DEFINER "list-my-rows-with-foreign-context" RPC skeleton.** Contract: `returns table(<flat columns including the cross-tenant bits>)`, `language sql stable security definer set search_path = ''`, body is a straight `select ... from public.<owned_table> t left join public.<foreign_table> f on ... where t.<owner_col> = public.current_user_id() order by ...`. Grants: `revoke all ... from public`, `revoke all ... from anon`, `grant execute ... to authenticated`. Service-role is intentionally not granted — it already has BYPASSRLS and wouldn't use this shape. Why `stable`, not `volatile`: the function reads but doesn't write, so PostgREST can plan around it and query planner caches can reuse results within a statement. Why `sql` over `plpgsql`: the body is one statement, no control flow, no GET STACKED DIAGNOSTICS — inlines better. Verified pattern via a transactional `begin; set local role authenticated; set local "request.jwt.claims" = '{"sub":"<uuid>","role":"creator"}'; select * from public.<fn>();` block that confirms (a) the DEFINER bypass returns the cross-tenant columns, (b) the ownership check still gates other users' rows (swap the sub claim to a different creator and assert zero rows). Ref: `supabase/migrations/20260418010100_us_043_list_my_applications_rpc.sql`. Applies to any future "my X with lister/creator/reviewer context" list screen.
- **Screen-RPC naming convention: `public.list_my_<things>` for caller-scoped list reads, `public.<action>_rpc` for mutations, `public.<thing>_<metric>(arg)` for aggregates.** Precedents: `public.list_my_applications()` (US-043) / `public.apply_to_listing_rpc(...)` (US-041) / `public.submission_reuse_count(uuid)` (US-009). Keep the `list_my_*` functions argumentless (the caller is implicit via `current_user_id()`) — passing a caller UUID would re-introduce the spoofing surface the helpers exist to eliminate. Keep them in the `public` schema (not `app_private`) because they need to be callable via PostgREST `supabase.rpc(...)`; `app_private` is reserved for server-side triggers + cron bodies. The `authenticated` grant is mandatory — without it PostgREST returns 42501 "permission denied for function" and the MCP `execute_sql` test path (runs as `postgres`) silently passes, masking the bug.
- **Segmented control a11y: `accessibilityRole="tablist"` wrapper + `accessibilityRole="tab"` on each button + `accessibilityState={{ selected }}`**, NOT the Chip primitive's `role="checkbox"` + `checked`. Chip is for multi-select filter rows where each pill is independent; a segmented control is single-select and semantically is a tab bar. iOS VoiceOver reads "tab, Pending, selected, 1 of 4" for the tablist shape — correct under Apple's a11y guidance. `selected` is the right state (not `checked`) because the user isn't toggling a boolean, they're picking one of N mutually-exclusive views. Inline the control rather than promoting to a shared primitive until a second screen needs it — premature abstraction hurts more than a ~40-line duplicate. Ref: `SegmentControl` in `app/(creator)/applications.tsx` (US-043).
- **Enum-to-UI-bucket maps live as a static `Record<DBEnum, UISegment>` next to the screen that owns the mapping, not in a shared constants file.** `application_status` has 6 values; the Applied tab shows 4 segments (pending / approved / rejected / cancelled), with `withdrawn + cancelled_listing_edit + cancelled_listing_closed` all bucketing to "cancelled" per design.md §3.1 label + the AC's 4-segment design. Type the map as `Record<Database['public']['Enums']['application_status'], Segment>` so adding a 7th enum value trips tsc at the map literal. Do NOT collapse the three cancellation variants in the DB (they carry distinct semantic meaning for future admin/analytics use); collapse only at the UI layer. Same pattern applies to any status enum whose DB shape is richer than the screen's tab count. Ref: `STATUS_BUCKET` in `app/(creator)/applications.tsx`.
- **Two-phase verification probe pattern for screens whose populated case isn't reachable from the in-app DevRolePicker.** The DevRolePicker mints a literal `dev-creator-token` string that PostgREST rejects with PGRST301; only `auth-signup-creator`-issued JWTs are accepted. For screens that need populated-row screenshots (US-043 list-my-applications, future US-047 my-submissions), the project precedent is: (1) take empty-state mobile-mcp screenshots from the DevRolePicker session to validate layout + segment switching + each empty copy, (2) run a two-phase Bun probe that signs up a fresh creator (mode=signup → prints `{user_id, token}`), pauses for the operator to seed fixtures via Supabase MCP execute_sql, then (mode=query) calls the screen's RPC with the captured JWT and asserts the contract holds end-to-end through PostgREST. The probe **must mirror the screen's bucket-collapse map exactly** (typed `Record<EnumValue, Bucket>`, not a chained ternary with an implicit else) so a future enum addition trips tsc on both sides. Always delete the probe fixture (user + applications + cascading social_links/creator_profiles/metric_snapshots) via execute_sql immediately after the probe finishes; the user row is not auto-cascaded. Ref: `scripts/test/probe-us043-list-apps.ts`. Same posture US-036 took for the gesture-blocked refresh test.
- **Realtime `postgres_changes` without a server-side filter when the table lacks a direct caller-id column.** `applications` has `creator_id` so US-044's subscription filters on `creator_id=eq.<uuid>`; `submissions` has no such column (ownership runs through `submissions.application_id → applications.creator_id`). For tables gated via a join, OMIT the filter clause entirely and let the broker's RLS evaluation (`<table>_creator_rw` / `_lister_read` policies) gate visibility server-side. The alternative — passing `application_id=in.(...)` — would require the client to maintain the id set, re-subscribe on every new owned row, and race the RLS evaluation; strictly worse than letting RLS do its job. Keep the client-side row-match guard (`rowsRef.current.find((r) => r.id === next.id)`) so updates for rows that aren't currently surfaced by the RPC are ignored cheaply. Applies to any child-of-join table: `submission_videos`, future `review_threads`, etc. Ref: `app/(creator)/submissions.tsx` US-047 realtime effect.
- **Supabase Realtime tenant hibernation is an infrastructure-class blocker, not a code defect.** When `supabase.realtime.channel(...).subscribe()` returns `TIMED_OUT` repeatedly (4+ times) and `get_logs({service:'realtime'})` shows `UnableToConnectToProject: Realtime was unable to connect to the project database` every 5-15s, the project's Realtime tenant is stuck in an initialization loop that can stretch `Realtime.Tenants.Connect.CheckConnection` past 8 minutes before the broker gives up and the subscription never reaches `SUBSCRIBED`. The HTTP side (PostgREST, edge functions, `execute_sql`) stays healthy throughout — this is purely a realtime-broker-to-replication-slot connectivity issue. The `pg_stat_activity` view shows realtime's own connections as `idle`, giving a false-positive "it's connected" signal; trust the realtime logs' `UnableToConnectToProject` entries over `pg_stat_activity`. For realtime-dependent stories (US-044), the decision rule: if empty-state mobile-mcp screenshots + code-reviewer pass + RLS/publication membership verified at DB level, ship the implementation under `passes:false` with a detailed blocker note + ready-to-run probe, rather than looping indefinitely. Next iteration re-runs the probe once logs show `Starting stream replication for slot supabase_realtime_replication_slot_v2_83_2` (the postgres_changes slot, distinct from the `_messages_` slot that handles broadcast) without a trailing `UnableToConnectToProject`. Before investigating any realtime test failure, check these logs FIRST — client-side debugging is wasted effort when the broker can't reach Postgres. Ref: `scripts/test/probe-us044-realtime.ts` + this iteration's US-044 blocker notes.
- **Caller-scoped dashboard aggregate RPC beats N round-trips.** When a screen needs multiple `count(*)` aggregates all gated by the same caller-scoped filter (Lister Home: active campaigns / pending applications / pending submissions), expose ONE `SECURITY INVOKER` SQL function returning a typed row with all the counts rather than N PostgREST `.select('*', { count: 'exact', head: true })` calls. Three wins: (1) atomic — the numbers come from the same snapshot, no race where they disagree because they were fetched 20ms apart; (2) typed via `Database['public']['Functions']['<name>']['Returns'][number]` so the caller reads column names, not Range-header counts; (3) one network hop. Repeat the `<fkcol> = public.current_user_id()` filter inside EVERY sub-select even though RLS already applies — belt-and-suspenders so a future RLS regression doesn't silently inflate counts with other callers' rows. Pattern #42 still applies: `set search_path = ''` + schema-qualify enum casts (`'active'::public.listing_status`). Ref: `lister_dashboard_counts` in `us_048_lister_dashboard_counts_rpc.sql`.
- **`allowFontScaling={false}` vs `maxFontSizeMultiplier={1.3}` — they are NOT interchangeable.** Per docs/design.md §1.3 + §11 QA checklist: CONTENT text (body, captions, display-size numbers in stat tiles, card titles, anything informational) must honor system Dynamic Type up to 130% via `maxFontSizeMultiplier={1.3}`. UI CHROME (pills, chips, tab-bar labels, segment buttons — fixed-sized containers where scaling would break layout) opts out entirely with `allowFontScaling={false}`. The trap: a hero metric using `textStyles.display` LOOKS like chrome (big, standalone, inside a tile) but is CONTENT — the whole point of the screen. Getting this wrong makes the app inaccessible to users with large system text without any compile-time signal. Reviewer checklist: for every Text component, ask "does the meaning come from the value shown?" — if yes, `maxFontSizeMultiplier={1.3}`; if the text is a static UI label on a fixed-geometry element, `allowFontScaling={false}`. Ref: `StatTile` in `app/(lister)/dashboard.tsx`.
- **Zero-data state and empty-state are distinct UI states even when they share the same data shape.** A dashboard that renders `0 / 0 / 0` across three tiles when the user has nothing to show is a UX miss — design.md §5 specifies dedicated empty-state copy for every screen (Lister dashboard: "Post your first bounty."). The engineering rule: after a successful load, check if the data is semantically empty (all counts zero, empty list, etc.) and branch to the §5 copy BEFORE rendering the populated layout. Don't conflate "API returned successfully" with "there is data worth showing". Pattern applies to any counts-aggregate screen (creator earnings dashboard, lister analytics in v2, etc.) — each gets its own §5 empty-state copy, not a generic "No data." fallback. Ref: `isAllZero(counts)` branch in `app/(lister)/dashboard.tsx`.
- **Functional-updater context methods for state whose next value depends on the previous.** A plain `setField(key, value)` API forces callers to compute `value` from a closure-captured snapshot (`[...draft.preConditions, row]`), which stale-closes on rapid-fire handlers (two taps in one render tick both see the pre-first-tap array). The fix at the context level is an `updateField(key, (prev) => next)` variant that threads the functional updater all the way down to React's `setState` — then the callback reads the latest value at commit time, not at bind time. Every wizard-like store (WizardStore, future onboarding store) should expose both shapes: `setField` for scalar writes where prev is irrelevant, `updateField` for arrays / counters / anything derived. The pattern complements Codebase Pattern #106 (synchronous ref locks for the tap side); updateField handles the state side, the ref lock handles the tap-dedup side, and using them together is belt-and-suspenders. Ref: `src/screens/campaign-wizard/WizardStore.tsx` `updateField` (US-050).
- **`<TextInput maxLength={N}>` only caps direct keyboard input — pasted text bypasses it.** On iOS especially, paste/autofill can deliver a longer string than `maxLength` allows; the store ends up holding a value that is longer than the visible input will ever display (render side gets capped, write side does not). For any numeric/length-capped text field that must stay in sync with what the user sees, ADD a parallel `if (next.length > N) return;` guard inside the `onChangeText` handler BEFORE the regex/parse step. `maxLength` still helps on the keyboard path (prevents the keystroke from registering) and `hitSlop`/accessibility remain correct, so keep both — they're complementary, not redundant. Applies to: max_submissions, threshold, price-major inputs, any digit-capped TextInput. Ref: `onChangeMax` guard in `app/(lister)/campaigns/new/step-4.tsx` (US-052).
- **Re-use client-side validation helpers across client AND server paths instead of re-declaring regexes.** When a validation helper (e.g. `classifyVideoUrl` in `src/lib/oembed.ts`) already encodes the canonical TikTok/Instagram URL shapes for one path (US-046 submission composer), every other surface that validates the same URL shape — wizard fields, form previews, server-side edge functions — must import it directly rather than inlining its own regex. Single-sourcing means that when TikTok changes a URL shape or when the server adds a new permitted variant (e.g. shortlinks), one file moves and every consumer agrees. Rule of thumb: if you catch yourself writing `/^https:\/\/(?:www\.)?(?:tiktok|instagram)/` in a new file, stop — import the helper that already exists. Applies to any semantic-validation helper in `src/lib/` (URL classifiers, currency parsers, handle validators). Ref: step-4's import of `classifyVideoUrl` from `@/lib/oembed` matches the submission composer's (US-046) usage.
- **Validator numeric caps must match the DB column type, not `Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER`.** An edge function validator that accepts values larger than the PG column's type produces user-hostile 500 DB_ERROR responses when the RPC's silent `::integer` / `::smallint` cast overflows inside the transaction — the correct outcome is a 400 INVALID_REQUEST at the validation boundary. Marketify caps: `int` columns at `2_147_483_647` (e.g. `pre_conditions.threshold` feeding `min_followers_*`), `smallint` at `32_767`, and any domain cap (e.g. `price_cents` ≤ 1e9, `max_submissions` ≤ 99_999) tighter where it makes sense. Any new edge function that forwards numeric inputs to a PG column of a narrower type must cap at the PG type's max. Ref: `PRE_THRESHOLD_MAX` in `supabase/functions/create-listing/index.ts` (US-053).
- **Decouple post-success client-side cleanup from the success signal.** After a server write commits (listing published, submission approved, purchase cleared), client-side cleanup tasks (AsyncStorage.removeItem, cache invalidation, analytics ping) must NOT be awaited before the toast + navigation. Pattern: `showToast(...); router.replace(...); void cleanup().catch(err => console.error(...));` — awaiting cleanup first turns any flaky-storage error into a false-negative "Couldn't publish" toast that invites the user to re-submit, duplicating the server-side write. Applies to wizard publish flows, in-app purchase receipts, offline-queue drains, anywhere the server has already committed. Ref: `publish` handler in `app/(lister)/campaigns/new/step-5.tsx` (US-053, landed after code-reviewer CRITICAL finding).
- **DevRolePicker is the canonical dev-only Marketify JWT mint.** `app/(auth)/index.tsx` DevRolePicker calls `auth-signup-{creator,lister}` edge functions with throwaway `dev_<role>_<Date.now()>` handles to mint real Marketify HS256 JWTs. Any edge function that enforces `verifyJwt(token)` + `app_role` checks (create-listing, submit-video, etc.) requires a real session — do NOT invent a new fixture path. The bypass is guarded by `__DEV__` so it strips from production; the timestamped handle avoids USERNAME_TAKEN collisions across rapid-fire mobile-mcp runs. Ref: `DevRolePicker` in `app/(auth)/index.tsx` (updated in US-053).
- **Confirmations are centered modals, not bottom sheets.** docs/design.md has two distinct modal idioms. §3.3 says "modals (except confirmations) are bottom sheets", and §5.2 line 635 carves confirmations out as centered cards. When building a destructive-action confirm (cascade cancel, delete, publish), use a plain RN `<Modal transparent animationType="fade">` with a 360-wide centered card; do NOT reuse `BottomSheet`. The neubrutalist language (2px ink border, `shadows.hard`, pill buttons) stays consistent. Ref: `CascadeConfirmModal` in `app/(lister)/campaigns/[id]/edit.tsx` (US-055).
- **Cascade logic lives in the DB trigger, not the client.** `app_private.bump_listing_version` (us_010) is the single source of truth for which listing fields version-bump (`price_cents`, `currency`, `max_submissions` scalars + the ephemeral `version_bump_reason` column for conditions/sample_videos) and which application rows flip to `cancelled_listing_edit` on commit. Any UI that touches this flow is strictly: (1) count pending apps for the warning modal, (2) issue the PATCH, (3) toast. Don't re-declare which fields cascade in JS — read the trigger if unsure. Ref: `supabase/migrations/20260417150000_us_010_cascade_trigger.sql` + US-055 edit screen.
- **Ownership-transitive counts work via direct PostgREST under existing RLS — no RPC wrapper needed.** When the lister needs to count rows on a related table that is RLS-scoped through a transitive ownership path (e.g. `applications` → `listings.lister_id = current_user_id()` via `applications_lister_read`), use `supabase.from('<table>').select('id', {head:true, count:'exact'}).eq(...)` directly. The policy already enforces ownership. No `SECURITY DEFINER` function needed unless you're crossing tenants (see the `submission_reuse_count` pattern above for the cross-tenant case). Ref: pending-app count in `app/(lister)/campaigns/[id]/edit.tsx` `onSave` (US-055).
- **When seeding test listings manually, insert the v1 `listing_versions` row in the same statement.** The app's write paths (create-listing edge function) create a v1 `listing_versions` record alongside the `listings` insert, so `applications.listing_version_id` has a non-null FK target. A raw `INSERT INTO listings ...` via execute_sql bypasses this, and subsequent `INSERT INTO applications ...` fails with `null value in column "listing_version_id"`. Cleanup order matters too: delete `applications` first, then `listings` (which FKs to `current_version_id`), then `listing_versions`. Used for US-055 cascade e2e probe.

Each card: campaign title (listing.title → version snapshot title → "Untitled campaign"), `@lister_handle`, StatusPill, "Applied {formatRelativeTime}" — tap routes to `/(creator)/listing/{listing_id}`. 4-segment UI mapped from the 6-value `application_status` enum via a typed `STATUS_BUCKET: Record<ApplicationStatus, Segment>` (withdrawn + cancelled_listing_edit + cancelled_listing_closed → 'cancelled').
- New migration `supabase/migrations/20260418010100_us_043_list_my_applications_rpc.sql`: SECURITY DEFINER SQL function `public.list_my_applications()` returns table of flat joined rows (id, status, created_at, listing_id, listing_title, lister_handle, version_title). Filters by `a.creator_id = public.current_user_id()`. Grants: revoke all from public/anon, grant execute to authenticated. Required because a PostgREST embed `.select('..., lister:users!lister_id(username), version:listing_versions!...(snapshot)')` would silently NULL `lister_handle` for every row (users RLS is self-only) and the version snapshot for non-active listings (listing_versions RLS gates on listing.status='active' OR caller=lister).
- New seed migration `supabase/migrations/20260418010000_us_043_seed_applications.sql`: 4 deterministic-UUID applications for the seed creator — pending/Acme, approved/Luxe, rejected/Acme, cancelled_listing_edit/Luxe — sized so the partial unique `applications_open_uniq(listing_id, creator_id) WHERE status IN ('pending','approved')` does not collide (pending+rejected can coexist on Acme; approved+cancelled_listing_edit can coexist on Luxe because the partial index excludes `cancelled_listing_edit`). Idempotent via `on conflict (id) do nothing`.
- Verified via SQL under a simulated creator JWT (`begin; set local role authenticated; set local "request.jwt.claims" = '{"sub":"11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111002","role":"creator"}'; select * from public.list_my_applications();`) — returns all 4 rows with populated listing_title + lister_handle + version_title, confirming the DEFINER bypass works and the ownership check still gates.

  - `supabase/migrations/20260418010000_us_043_seed_applications.sql` (new)

Code remains review-ready for when the dashboard fix lands.
- Code-reviewer verdict: issues fixed in this commit — (1) (confidence 92) original PostgREST embed `lister:users!lister_id(username)` would silently null `lister_handle` for every row under the self-only `users_self_select` policy; fixed by switching to the SECURITY DEFINER RPC that joins under its own ownership check. (2) (confidence 85) `listing_versions_read_if_listing_readable` gates on `listings.status='active'` so the snapshot title would null for cancelled-listing-edit / rejected-against-closed rows; same RPC fix covers this. Both findings rated IMPORTANT; no findings rated CRITICAL or dismissed.
- Spec gap: docs/product-plan.md + docs/design.md:151 call the tab `/(creator)/applications`; the US-043 AC text says `app/(creator)/applied.tsx`. Docs win — matches the canonical URL already wired into US-031's TabBar. The tab label "Applied" is preserved.
- JWT drift blocker still in effect: `bun run scripts/test/probe-jwt-drift.ts` returns 401 PGRST301 "No suitable key or wrong key type". Every screen that reads from PostgREST under the custom Marketify JWT remains unverifiable on-device until Project Settings → API → JWT Settings is re-aligned with `MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET` (human-performed dashboard action, not executable from Ralph).
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - **Audit PostgREST embeds against the target table's RLS BEFORE writing the .select().** The silent-nullout failure mode is high-damage low-visibility: no error, no warning, just a `null` field. The checklist for this project specifically: `users!<fk>` cross-tenant reads are always silent-nulled by `users_self_select`; `listing_versions!<fk>` reads are silent-nulled for non-active listings; `listings!<fk>(title)` reads are silent-nulled for non-active listings. If your screen needs ANY of those cross-tenant fields, you need a DEFINER RPC, not an embed. Promoted to Codebase Patterns.
  - **The "list my rows with foreign context" RPC is a reusable skeleton.** argumentless, SQL-language, STABLE, DEFINER, `set search_path=''`, left-joined, filtered by `current_user_id()`, granted to authenticated only. Copy this skeleton for US-056 (creator inbox), US-054 (lister campaigns list), US-057 (lister applicants list) — all three have the same cross-tenant-read-under-self-only-RLS shape. Promoted to Codebase Patterns.
  - **Segmented controls are tablists, not checkbox groups.** When a control is single-select + mutually-exclusive, `accessibilityRole="tablist"` + per-button `"tab"` + `accessibilityState={{ selected }}` is the correct a11y shape. The Chip primitive's `role="checkbox"` + `state={{ checked }}` is only correct for multi-select filters. Promoted to Codebase Patterns.
  - **DB enums can outgrow UI segments — a static typed Record bridges them.** `application_status` has 6 values; the tab shows 4 segments. A `Record<ApplicationStatus, Segment>` literal with all 6 keys explicitly enumerated means tsc catches the day a 7th enum value lands; collapsing the DB shape to match the UI would lose the distinction between user-initiated withdrawal and lister-initiated cancellation. Keep the DB rich, collapse at the UI. Promoted to Codebase Patterns.
  - **Skipping `passes:true` because of an upstream env blocker is the right call.** US-036 + US-038 + now US-043 are all mature implementations waiting on a dashboard-side JWT secret alignment. Pretending they're "done" would corrupt the progress signal for the human reviewer; pretending they're "not started" would lose the implementation work. The `passes:false` + detailed notes pattern (with re-run probe script + rationale) is how we keep the ledger honest. Same posture will apply to any future story whose verification path goes through PostgREST under the creator/lister JWT until the drift is resolved.

lines: -      "notes": "2026-04-18: Implementation complete + code-reviewed, but E2E realtime delivery verification BLOCKED by Supabase Realtime tenant infrastructure failure for project [REDACTED_SUPABASE_PROJECT_REF]. Server-side realtime logs show persistent 'UnableToConnectToProject: Realtime was unable to connect to the project database' every 5-15s (even after the probe stops), with one prior 'Realtime.Tenants.Connect.CheckConnection executed in 494396 ms' (~8min) suggesting a stuck tenant cold-start path \u2014 this is beyond what the client can recover from. Work shipped this iteration: (1) migration us_044_applications_realtime_publication adds public.applications to supabase_realtime (default replica identity is sufficient because the client only reads payload.new.id+status); (2) app/(creator)/applications.tsx extended with a useEffect that calls supabase.realtime.setAuth(getCachedToken()) then subscribes to UPDATE events on applications filtered by creator_id=eq.<me>, patches rows in state on a status change, and fires the AC-specified success/error toast ('Your application was approved/rejected') for approved and rejected only (other 4 enum values patch the row silently since AC only names the two decisions); (3) a useRef mirror of `rows` (Codebase Pattern #82/#106) prevents stale-closure reads inside the subscription callback without forcing re-subscription on every list mutation; (4) mobile-mcp captured us-044-applied-pending-empty.png + us-044-applied-approved-empty.png proving the subscription wiring doesn't crash the screen under the DevRolePicker creator session (subscription is no-op there because the dev token is a literal string, not a signed JWT \u2014 same posture as US-036/US-043 per Codebase Pattern #122); (5) scripts/test/probe-us044-realtime.ts two-mode probe (signup | subscribe) that mints a real Marketify JWT via auth-signup-creator, calls supabase.realtime.setAuth(jwt), subscribes to UPDATE events under the same RLS policy (applications_creator_rw, polcmd='*') that guards the in-app stream, and asserts payload.new.status matches expected \u2014 ran end-to-end this iteration and returned FAIL with '[channel] status=TIMED_OUT' repeated four times because the broker never reached SUBSCRIBED. Probe test fixture (user_id a3495acd-7241-4d41-8e15-d42a210fdf65 + cascading rows) was cleaned up immediately via execute_sql. Code-reviewer returned 2 IMPORTANT findings, both carried over unchanged from US-036 (stale-token-at-subscription-time baseline across all realtime screens) and both AC-conformant (toast fires only on approved/rejected, matching the exact AC wording). Next iteration should re-run the probe (bun run scripts/test/probe-us044-realtime.ts signup then subscribe) once Supabase Realtime tenant is healthy \u2014 if verdict PASS, flip passes:true. Tenant hibernation/cold-start is the likely root cause; waiting for the tenant to warm up + checking get_logs({service:'realtime'}) for 'Starting stream replication for slot supabase_realtime_replication_slot_v2_83_2' (the postgres_changes slot, not just the messages slot) before running the probe is the recommended order. | 2026-04-18 06:43 re-verify: realtime tenant still broken \u2014 same TIMED_OUT pattern. New probe run signed up user 7ca930b8-...2297, seeded application 1d5d3800-...6350 against listing 11111111-...010, subscribed \u2192 4x TIMED_OUT then verdict FAIL. get_logs({service:realtime}) confirms only the broadcast slot supabase_realtime_messages_replication_slot_v2_83_2 ever starts; the postgres_changes slot supabase_realtime_replication_slot_v2_83_2 has not appeared in 24h. CheckConnection latency oscillates 476ms (healthy) \u2194 670s+ (cold-start) \u2194 tenant-shutdown-on-idle. No code change this iteration; fixtures cleaned up. Next iteration: re-run the probe as before; if get_logs shows the postgres_changes slot starting (search for slot name without the messages_ infix), the broker is finally healthy and the probe should reach SUBSCRIBED. | 2026-04-18 09:58 re-verify #3: tenant still broken. Per Codebase Pattern #124 decision rule, did NOT run the probe this iteration because get_logs({service:'realtime'}) STILL shows only the broadcast slot supabase_realtime_messages_replication_slot_v2_83_2 starting (never supabase_realtime_replication_slot_v2_83_2, the postgres_changes slot). Recent realtime log entries (last 24h window): 'Realtime.Tenants.Connect.CheckConnection executed in 672448 ms' + 'executed in 800322 ms' + 'executed in 672573 ms' (3 cold-starts each >11min), 'UnableToConnectToProject' flaring every 5-15s, tenant repeatedly hibernating ('Stop tenant [REDACTED_SUPABASE_PROJECT_REF] because of no connected users') \u2192 re-initializing \u2192 timing out again. The single 'executed in 476 ms' in the window was immediately followed by another hibernation cycle. Skipping probe execution saves a fresh probe-user fixture + cleanup cycle that would predictably TIMED_OUT; the get_logs observation is authoritative per the established pattern. Code unchanged. Next iteration: re-check get_logs first; only run the probe once you see 'Starting stream replication for slot supabase_realtime_replication_slot_v2_83_2' (WITHOUT the _messages_ infix) followed by absence of 'UnableToConnectToProject' for at least 60s. Supabase-side dashboard action (tenant restart / support ticket) may be required if the pattern persists into iteration #4+."

content.text: Now I need to design US-056. Let me delegate an Explore pass so I understand the lister-side layout, the Applications tab structure (US-043 precedent), the applications + creator_profiles shape, and what inbox routing already exists. This story touches >3 files.

input.description: Research US-056 Lister Inbox Applications context
input.prompt:
You are scoping US-056 for the Marketify Ralph loop. The story is:

**US-056 — Lister Inbox: Applications tab**
- `app/(lister)/inbox/applications.tsx` queries `applications` joined to `listings` + `creator_profiles`
- Each row shows creator handles (mono), TikTok followers, IG followers + avg views, application pitch preview
- Realtime subscription for inserts
- Mobile-mcp verification: screenshot
- Commit

I need to understand the existing scaffolding so I can plan the minimal implementation. Please return a concise (under 500 words) report covering:

1. **Lister tab layout** — how does `app/(lister)/_layout.tsx` work and what tabs does `src/components/shared/TabBar.tsx` define for the lister? Is there an "Inbox" tab already? If so, what does `app/(lister)/inbox.tsx` or `app/(lister)/inbox/*.tsx` look like today?

2. **Existing creator-side Applied tab (US-043 precedent)** — `app/(creator)/applications.tsx` is the closest analog. Briefly sketch its structure: segmented control, RPC call, row card, realtime subscription pattern. Flag any helpers / primitives it uses (StatusPill, SegmentControl inline, formatRelativeTime, etc.). This will be my template shape.

3. **Schema — which columns do `applications`, `listings`, `creator_profiles`, `users`, `social_links` actually expose?** I need creator TikTok followers (`creator_profiles.tiktok_follower_count`), IG followers (`instagram_follower_count`), IG avg views last 10 (`instagram_avg_views_last_10`), creator username (`users.username`), creator handle (via social_links on the two platforms), and the application pitch text. Report exact column names from `src/types/supabase.ts` (or docs/tech-architecture.md §4.7) — don't guess.

4. **RLS posture** — what policy gates `applications` reads by the lister? Is there already an `applications_lister_read` or similar policy? Does it join through `listings.lister_id`? Same question for `creator_profiles` — can the lister read another user's creator_profiles row directly, or will we hit the silent-null embed trap from Pattern #117 and need a SECURITY DEFINER RPC (like `list_my_applications` from US-043)?

5. **Realtime publication state** — is `public.applications` already in `supabase_realtime` publication? US-044's migration added it; report whether that migration exists (`us_044_applications_realtime_publication`) and what tables it publishes.

6. **Seed data** — are there seeded applications from US-043's `us_043_seed_applications` migration that a seeded lister user (from US-033/US-055 dev flow) would see on this inbox? Report the listing_ids those seeds target and whether the seed lister owns them.

7. **Existing lister screens that already call screen-scoped RPCs** — `app/(lister)/dashboard.tsx` uses `lister_dashboard_counts`, and any campaigns/inbox screens already use similar RPCs. I want a short list of what's already wired so I don't duplicate anything.

Do NOT implement anything. Do NOT suggest changes. Just map the current state. Use file:line pointers wherever possible so I can jump straight to the relevant code in the implementation phase.

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile a concise report:

## US-056 Scaffolding Report — Lister Inbox: Applications Tab

### 1. **Lister Tab Layout**

**File:** `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/app/(lister)/_layout.tsx` (lines 8–42)

The lister has 4 tabs via `LISTER_TABS`:
- Home (LayoutDashboard) → `/(lister)/dashboard`
- Campaigns (Megaphone) → `/(lister)/campaigns`
- **Inbox (Inbox icon)** → `/(lister)/inbox` ✓ Already wired
- Profile (Building2) → `/(lister)/profile`

**Current Inbox State:** `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/app/(lister)/inbox.tsx` (lines 1–12)
Shows a `<TabPlaceholder>` with text "Applications + Submissions tabs land in US-056 and US-058." — **no subdirectory structure exists yet.** The route group pattern `/(lister)/inbox` will route to this file; US-056 requires refactoring it to either:
- Keep `inbox.tsx` as a tab-switcher (hosting a segmented control), or
- Create `inbox/_layout.tsx` + `inbox/applications.tsx` + `inbox/submissions.tsx` sub-files

The existing `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/src/components/shared/TabBar.tsx` (lines 1–93) drives tab navigation via `usePathname()` + `isActive()` helper; no specialized sub-tab bar exists yet. Precedent: `app/(creator)/applications.tsx` uses an inline `<SegmentControl>` (see §2 below).

---

### 2. **Creator-Side Applied Tab (US-043 Precedent)**

**File:** `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/app/(creator)/applications.tsx` (lines 24–258)

**Structure sketch:**
- **RPC call** (line 142): `supabase.rpc('list_my_applications')` → flat row array
- **Inline SegmentControl** (lines 77–117): single-select `pending|approved|rejected|cancelled` via `SegmentButton` pressables + `STATUS_BUCKET` mapping
- **Row bucketing** (lines 212–223): useMemo splits rows by segment
- **Row card** (lines 286–324): `ApplicationRowCard` renders title (with fallback chain: listing title → version snapshot title → "Untitled"), lister handle (mono via `@`), status pill (color-coded), relative time
- **Realtime subscription** (lines 163–210): `supabase.channel()` listens for UPDATE events on `applications` table filtered by `creator_id=eq.<id>`, patches state on status change, fires success/error toast for `approved`/`rejected`
- **Helpers used:**
  - `StatusPill` (primitive, lines 289, 319)
  - `formatRelativeTime` (line 290, imported from `@/lib/time`)
  - `useToast()` (line 122)
  - `SkeletonCard` (line 235)

---

### 3. **Schema — Exact Column Names**

**Source:** `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/src/types/supabase.ts`

| Table | Column | Line(s) | Type |
|-------|--------|---------|------|
| `creator_profiles` | `tiktok_follower_count` | 89 | `number \| null` |
| `creator_profiles` | `instagram_follower_count` | 85 | `number \| null` |
| `creator_profiles` | `instagram_avg_views_last_10` | 84 | `number \| null` |
| `users` | `username` | 722 | `string` |
| `social_links` | `handle` | 570 | `string` |
| `social_links` | `platform` | 576 | `Enums["platform"]` (tiktok \| instagram \| youtube) |
| `applications` | `cover_note` | 19 | `string \| null` |
| `applications` | `id, listing_id, creator_id, status, created_at` | 20–28 | UUIDs + enum + timestamptz |
| `listings` | `title` | 331 | `string` |

**Handle resolution:** Social links are not directly embedded in creator_profiles; fetch via RLS-gated `social_links` rows per creator (`applications.creator_id` → `users.id` → `social_links.user_id`) for each platform (tiktok + instagram). The `list_my_applications` RPC returns only the lister's handle; a lister-facing RPC for applications will need to JOIN social_links to fetch creator handles.

---

### 4. **RLS Posture for Lister Access**

**File:** `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/supabase/migrations/20260417140000_us_009_rls_policies.sql` (lines 197–228)

| Policy | Table | Effect |
|--------|-------|--------|
| `applications_lister_read` | `applications` | **SELECT** allowed if `EXISTS (SELECT FROM listings l WHERE l.id = applications.listing_id AND l.lister_id = current_user_id())` |
| `applications_lister_decide` | `applications` | **UPDATE** allowed (same EXISTS gate) |
| `creator_profiles_lister_read` | `creator_profiles` | **SELECT** allowed if `EXISTS (SELECT FROM applications a JOIN listings l WHERE a.creator_id = creator_profiles.user_id AND l.lister_id = current_user_id())` |
| `social_links_lister_read` | `social_links` | **SELECT** allowed if `EXISTS (SELECT FROM applications a JOIN listings l WHERE a.creator_id = social_links.user_id AND l.lister_id = current_user_id())` |

**No `list_lister_applications` RPC exists yet.** Like US-043, we'll likely need a **SECURITY DEFINER RPC** (Pattern #118) because:
- Plain PostgREST embed would need a multi-table JOIN through `applications` → `listings` → `creator_profiles` + `social_links`, filtered by lister ownership
- RLS allows lister read on `applications`, `creator_profiles`, and `social_links` only via EXISTS subqueries, which work for filtering but make a clean left-join shape harder (the RLS gates are per-row, not per-result-set)
- For cleaner shape + stability, mirror `list_my_applications` pattern: SECURITY DEFINER + explicit `lister_id = current_user_id()` gate via join

---

### 5. **Realtime Publication State**

**File:** `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/supabase/migrations/20260418030000_us_044_applications_realtime_publication.sql`

Migration exists ✓ and adds `public.applications` to `supabase_realtime` publication (line 8). **RLS enforcement** is applied by the Realtime broker via `supabase.realtime.setAuth(token)` — the client-side filter `filter: 'creator_id=eq.<id>'` is a throughput guard, not the security boundary.

The creator's `applications_creator_rw` RLS policy (`for all, using creator_id = current_user_id()`) restricts the stream. **For lister**, the stream would use `applications_lister_read` instead (same exists-gate, different column).

---

### 6. **Seed Data**

**File:** `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/supabase/migrations/20260418010000_us_043_seed_applications.sql`

Four deterministic applications seeded for creator `11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111002` (the seed creator from US-033/US-034 flow):
- **Pending** (3 hours ago) against Acme listing `11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111010`
- **Approved** (2 days ago) against Luxe listing `22222222-2222-2222-2222-222222222010`
- **Rejected** (5 days ago) against Acme
- **Cancelled** (7 days ago) against Luxe

**Lister ownership:** Both listing UUIDs belong to the seed lister (no explicit seed migration for lister's listings checked, but the Luxe listing is referenced in US-055 seed migrations). A seeded lister user from US-033/US-055 flow **will see these four applications** in the Applications tab (once created) because the RLS policy gates on `listings.lister_id = current_user_id()`.

---

### 7. **Existing Lister Screen-Scoped RPCs**

1. **`lister_dashboard_counts()`** — `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/supabase/migrations/20260418030000_us_048_lister_dashboard_counts_rpc.sql`
   - Returns: `active_campaigns, pending_applications, pending_submissions` (counts only)
   - Called by: `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/app/(lister)/dashboard.tsx` (line 64)
   - SECURITY INVOKER (simple aggregates, RLS handles filtering)

2. **`list_my_campaigns()`** — `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/supabase/migrations/20260418070000_us_054_list_my_campaigns_rpc.sql`
   - Returns: listings + per-row `applications_count` + `submissions_count`
   - Called by: `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/app/(lister)/campaigns.tsx` (line 142)
   - SECURITY DEFINER (Pattern #118: ownership check + stable count semantics)

**No applications-list RPC exists for lister yet;** US-056 will add `list_my_applications_as_lister()` or similar (following `list_my_campaigns` + `list_my_applications` naming convention).

---

## Summary Table

| Component | File | Status |
|-----------|------|--------|
| Lister layout + Inbox tab | `app/(lister)/_layout.tsx:29–33` | ✓ Route exists, placeholder screen |
| Placeholder content | `app/(lister)/inbox.tsx` | ✓ Exists, placeholder text |
| Creator-side Applications (precedent) | `app/(creator)/applications.tsx` | ✓ Complete, full template |
| Schema: applications | `src/types/supabase.ts:17–76` | ✓ Defined |
| Schema: creator_profiles | `src/types/supabase.ts:78–139` | ✓ Includes tiktok/ig counts |
| Schema: social_links | `src/types/supabase.ts:566–618` | ✓ Defined |
| RLS: applications_lister_read | `supabase/migrations/.../us_009_rls_policies.sql:203–211` | ✓ Exists |
| RLS: creator_profiles_lister_read | `supabase/migrations/.../us_009_rls_policies.sql:83–93` | ✓ Exists |
| RLS: social_links_lister_read | `supabase/migrations/.../us_009_rls_policies.sql:109–118` | ✓ Exists |
| Realtime publication | `supabase/migrations/.../us_044_applications_realtime_publication.sql` | ✓ Exists, applications added |
| Seed applications | `supabase/migrations/.../us_043_seed_applications.sql` | ✓ 4 rows against 2 seed listings |
| Lister RPC for applications | — | ❌ Needs to be created for US-056 |

12	-- / submissions_lister_read join through listings.lister_id), so INVOKER

17	-- authoritative gate. Revoke from anon/public; grant execute to authenticated.

31	-- consistent with "My Applications" recency-first ordering (US-043). Ties

-- US-054 — "My Campaigns" screen RPC.
--
-- Contract: `public.list_my_campaigns()` returns the caller's listings
-- (filtered to `lister_id = public.current_user_id()`) plus per-listing
-- aggregate counts for applications and submissions. Called by
-- `app/(lister)/campaigns.tsx` which buckets rows into two segments:
-- Active (status='active') vs Inactive (draft/paused/closed/archived).
--
-- Why SECURITY DEFINER + explicit ownership check (not SECURITY INVOKER):
-- the aggregate sub-selects traverse `applications` + `submissions` tables
-- that the lister has RLS read access to (polcmd='r' on applications_lister_read
-- / submissions_lister_read join through listings.lister_id), so INVOKER
-- would technically work today. DEFINER is chosen to mirror the US-043
-- `list_my_applications` precedent (Codebase Pattern #118) and to make
-- the count semantics stable regardless of future RLS tightening — the
-- function's own `where lister_id = current_user_id()` remains the single
-- authoritative gate. Revoke from anon/public; grant execute to authenticated.
--
-- Columns returned:
--   id, status — listings primary key + status enum (UI splits on this)
--   title — current title (listings.title, not the version snapshot; both
--     stay in sync via the bump trigger)
--   price_cents, currency — for CampaignCard footer
--   min_followers_tiktok, min_followers_instagram — cache columns populated
--     by the §15b threshold-refresh trigger; null when no threshold is set
--   applications_count — total applications against this listing, ALL statuses
--   submissions_count — total submissions against this listing, ALL statuses
--   created_at, updated_at — used for ordering
--
-- Ordering: `updated_at desc, created_at desc` — most recently edited first,
-- consistent with "My Applications" recency-first ordering (US-043). Ties
-- broken by creation order.
--
-- Codebase Patterns referenced:
--   #118 SECURITY DEFINER + ownership-check list-my-rows skeleton
--   #119 `list_my_<things>` naming convention; argumentless; public schema
--   #42  `set search_path = ''` + schema-qualify every user-defined ref
--   #39  reuses `public.current_user_id()` helper

create or replace function public.list_my_campaigns()
returns table (
  id                        uuid,
  status                    public.listing_status,
  title                     text,
  price_cents               integer,
  currency                  text,
  min_followers_tiktok      integer,
  min_followers_instagram   integer,
  applications_count        integer,
  submissions_count         integer,
  created_at                timestamptz,
  updated_at                timestamptz
)
language sql
stable
security definer
set search_path = ''
as $$
  select l.id,
         l.status,
         l.title,
         l.price_cents,
         l.currency,
         l.min_followers_tiktok,
         l.min_followers_instagram,
         (
           select count(*)::integer
           from public.applications a
           where a.listing_id = l.id
         ) as applications_count,
         (
           select count(*)::integer
           from public.submissions s
           join public.applications a2 on a2.id = s.application_id
           where a2.listing_id = l.id
         ) as submissions_count,
         l.created_at,
         l.updated_at
  from public.listings l
  where l.lister_id = public.current_user_id()
  order by l.updated_at desc, l.created_at desc;
$$;

revoke all on function public.list_my_campaigns() from public;
revoke all on function public.list_my_campaigns() from anon;
grant execute on function public.list_my_campaigns() to authenticated;

1	-- US-043 — SECURITY DEFINER function `list_my_applications()` that returns
2	-- the caller's applications joined to listings + users (lister handle) +
3	-- listing_versions (snapshot title). Created because the direct
4	-- PostgREST embed silently nulls `users.username` (users RLS is self-only)
5	-- and `listings`/`listing_versions` are both gated on listing.status =
6	-- 'active' — so cancelled / rejected-against-closed rows would render
7	-- without title or handle under a raw .select().
8	--
9	-- Ownership is enforced inside the function via public.current_user_id()
10	-- (JWT sub claim). Execute is granted to `authenticated` only; anon and
11	-- service_role are not granted (service_role already has BYPASSRLS and
12	-- wouldn't use this shape anyway).
13	--
14	-- Codebase pattern ref: progress.txt #40 (submission_reuse_count) and
15	-- #110 (apply_to_listing_rpc) — the SECURITY DEFINER-with-ownership-check
16	-- pattern for cross-tenant reads that cannot be expressed cleanly via
17	-- per-row RLS.
18	
19	create or replace function public.list_my_applications()
20	returns table (
21	  id              uuid,
22	  status          public.application_status,
23	  created_at      timestamptz,
24	  listing_id      uuid,
25	  listing_title   text,
26	  lister_handle   text,
27	  version_title   text
28	)
29	language sql
30	security definer
31	stable
32	set search_path = ''
33	as $$
34	  select a.id,
35	         a.status,
36	         a.created_at,
37	         a.listing_id,
38	         l.title as listing_title,
39	         u.username::text as lister_handle,
40	         (lv.snapshot ->> 'title') as version_title
41	  from public.applications a
42	  left join public.listings l         on l.id = a.listing_id
43	  left join public.users u            on u.id = l.lister_id
44	  left join public.listing_versions lv on lv.id = a.listing_version_id
45	  where a.creator_id = public.current_user_id()
46	  order by a.created_at desc;
47	$$;
48	
49	revoke all on function public.list_my_applications() from public;
50	revoke all on function public.list_my_applications() from anon;

file.content:
-- US-043 — SECURITY DEFINER function `list_my_applications()` that returns
-- the caller's applications joined to listings + users (lister handle) +
-- listing_versions (snapshot title). Created because the direct
-- PostgREST embed silently nulls `users.username` (users RLS is self-only)
-- and `listings`/`listing_versions` are both gated on listing.status =
-- 'active' — so cancelled / rejected-against-closed rows would render
-- without title or handle under a raw .select().
--
-- Ownership is enforced inside the function via public.current_user_id()
-- (JWT sub claim). Execute is granted to `authenticated` only; anon and
-- service_role are not granted (service_role already has BYPASSRLS and
-- wouldn't use this shape anyway).
--
-- Codebase pattern ref: progress.txt #40 (submission_reuse_count) and
-- #110 (apply_to_listing_rpc) — the SECURITY DEFINER-with-ownership-check
-- pattern for cross-tenant reads that cannot be expressed cleanly via
-- per-row RLS.

create or replace function public.list_my_applications()
returns table (
  id              uuid,
  status          public.application_status,
  created_at      timestamptz,
  listing_id      uuid,
  listing_title   text,
  lister_handle   text,
  version_title   text
)
language sql
security definer
stable
set search_path = ''
as $$
  select a.id,
         a.status,
         a.created_at,
         a.listing_id,
         l.title as listing_title,
         u.username::text as lister_handle,
         (lv.snapshot ->> 'title') as version_title
  from public.applications a
  left join public.listings l         on l.id = a.listing_id
  left join public.users u            on u.id = l.lister_id
  left join public.listing_versions lv on lv.id = a.listing_version_id
  where a.creator_id = public.current_user_id()
  order by a.created_at desc;
$$;

revoke all on function public.list_my_applications() from public;
revoke all on function public.list_my_applications() from anon;
grant execute on function public.list_my_applications() to authenticated;

-- US-043 — seed four applications for the seed creator so each segment
-- of the My Applications tab (Pending / Approved / Rejected / Cancelled)
-- renders with at least one row during mobile-mcp verification.
--
-- Partial unique `applications_open_uniq` is (listing_id, creator_id)
-- WHERE status IN ('pending','approved') so the four rows below do not
-- collide: one pending + one rejected against Acme, one approved + one
-- cancelled_listing_edit against Luxe. Rejected/cancelled are outside
-- the partial index and can coexist with pending/approved on the same
-- listing.
--
-- Idempotent via `on conflict (id) do nothing` — the deterministic UUIDs
-- below make a re-applied migration a no-op.

do $$
declare
  v_creator_id       uuid := '11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111002';  -- seed creator
  v_acme_listing_id  uuid := '11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111010';  -- Promote Acme Headphones
  v_acme_version_id  uuid := '11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111011';
  v_luxe_listing_id  uuid := '22222222-2222-2222-2222-222222222010';  -- Mega-Influencer Luxe Launch
  v_luxe_version_id  uuid := '22222222-2222-2222-2222-222222222011';

  v_app_pending_id   uuid := '33333333-3333-3333-3333-333333330001';
  v_app_approved_id  uuid := '33333333-3333-3333-3333-333333330002';
  v_app_rejected_id  uuid := '33333333-3333-3333-3333-333333330003';
  v_app_cancelled_id uuid := '33333333-3333-3333-3333-333333330004';
begin
  insert into public.applications (
    id, listing_id, listing_version_id, creator_id,
    status, cover_note, created_at, updated_at, decided_at, decision_note
  ) values
    (
      v_app_pending_id, v_acme_listing_id, v_acme_version_id, v_creator_id,
      'pending', 'Excited to try these headphones in my next travel vlog.',
      now() - interval '3 hours', now() - interval '3 hours', null, null
    ),
    (
      v_app_approved_id, v_luxe_listing_id, v_luxe_version_id, v_creator_id,
      'approved', null,
      now() - interval '2 days', now() - interval '1 day',
      now() - interval '1 day', 'Love your audience fit — green-lit.'
    ),
    (
      v_app_rejected_id, v_acme_listing_id, v_acme_version_id, v_creator_id,
      'rejected', null,
      now() - interval '5 days', now() - interval '4 days',
      now() - interval '4 days', 'Already have a similar creator on this drop.'
    ),
    (
      v_app_cancelled_id, v_luxe_listing_id, v_luxe_version_id, v_creator_id,
      'cancelled_listing_edit', null,
      now() - interval '7 days', now() - interval '6 days',
      now() - interval '6 days', null
    )
  on conflict (id) do nothing;
end $$;

-- US-040 — add a second active listing with high PRE thresholds so the
-- campaign-detail screen's ineligible state can be visually verified
-- against the seed creator (93.7M TikTok / 15k Instagram followers).
--
-- Thresholds chosen to fail BOTH platforms across multiple metrics so
-- the failed_conditions rail renders at least three rows:
--   * min_followers_tiktok        = 1_000_000_000  (actual 93_700_000 — fails)
--   * min_followers_instagram     = 50_000         (actual 15_000      — fails)
--   * instagram min_avg_views_last_n = 50_000      (actual 6_400       — fails)

do $$
declare
  v_lister_id  uuid := '11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111001';  -- reuse seed lister
  v_listing_id uuid := '22222222-2222-2222-2222-222222222010';
  v_version_id uuid := '22222222-2222-2222-2222-222222222011';
begin
  insert into public.listings (
    id, lister_id, status,
    title, description, category,
    price_cents, currency, max_submissions,
    min_followers_tiktok, min_followers_instagram,
    published_at
  ) values (
    v_listing_id, v_lister_id, 'active',
    'Mega-Influencer Luxe Launch',
    'A whitelist bounty for mega-channel creators only. Film a 45-60s launch reveal of our limited-run luxe drop. Bar is deliberately high to gate the ineligible UI state during QA.',
    'fashion',
    250000, 'USD', 3,
    1000000000, 50000,
    now()
  );

  insert into public.listing_versions (
    id, listing_id, version_number,
    price_cents, currency, max_submissions,
    snapshot, changed_fields
  ) values (
    v_version_id, v_listing_id, 1,
    250000, 'USD', 3,
    jsonb_build_object(
      'title',           'Mega-Influencer Luxe Launch',
      'price_cents',     250000,
      'currency',        'USD',
      'max_submissions', 3
    ),
    array[]::text[]
  );

  update public.listings
     set current_version_id = v_version_id
   where id = v_listing_id;

  insert into public.listing_conditions (
    listing_version_id, kind, metric, platform, operator, numeric_threshold
  ) values
    (v_version_id, 'pre', 'min_followers',        'tiktok',    'gte', 1000000000),
    (v_version_id, 'pre', 'min_followers',        'instagram', 'gte',      50000),
    (v_version_id, 'pre', 'min_avg_views_last_n', 'instagram', 'gte',      50000);

  insert into public.listing_conditions (
    listing_version_id, kind, metric, operator, bool_threshold
  ) values
    (v_version_id, 'post', 'post_family_friendly', 'bool', true);

  insert into public.listing_conditions (
    listing_version_id, kind, metric, operator, text_threshold
  ) values
    (v_version_id, 'post', 'post_must_mention', 'contains', '#LuxeDrop');

  insert into public.sample_videos (
    listing_version_id, platform, url, caption, sort_order
  ) values
    (v_version_id, 'tiktok',
     'https://www.tiktok.com/@acme_studio/video/7100000000000000002',
     'Launch reveal — reference A', 0),
    (v_version_id, 'instagram',
     'https://www.instagram.com/p/LuxeLaunchReel/',
     'Launch reveal — reference B', 1);
end $$;

content.content:
119-| Elevation | Two tiers: "flat" (no shadow) and "lifted" (hard shadow). No z-stacking beyond modal overlay. |
120:| Iconography | **Lucide React Native** exclusively. 24×24 viewBox, 2px stroke. Tab bar uses **stroke-weight toggle**, not filled variants — Lucide doesn't ship filled glyphs for every icon we need (`video`, `megaphone`, `inbox`). Inactive tab: `strokeWidth={1.5}`; active tab: `strokeWidth={2.5}` + fills the glyph's enclosed areas (e.g. compass needle) via a paired `fill` SVG we ship ourselves for the 4 creator + 4 lister tab icons. No emoji. |
121-| Imagery | Campaign sample videos get 2px ink border + hard shadow. Thumbnail aspect 9:16 for TikTok/Reels, 1:1 for avatars. |
122-| Empty-state illustrations | Geometric line drawings using primary/cta/ink only. No stock illustration libraries. |
123-| Haptics | `expo-haptics` — Light on press, Medium on status change, Success/Error on approve/reject arrivals. |
124-
--
157-| Edit Handles | `/(creator)/profile/handles` | Add / update / remove a TikTok or Instagram handle. Removing a handle stops future scrapes but preserves snapshot history. | "Save" |
158:| Notifications | `/(creator)/notifications` | Activity inbox | Tap item |
159-
160-### 2.3 Lister Screens
161-
162-| Screen | Route | Purpose | Primary CTA |
--
168-| Edit Campaign | `/(lister)/campaigns/[id]/edit` | Modify fields (triggers cancel-warning) | "Save changes" |
169:| Applications Inbox | `/(lister)/campaigns/[id]/applications` | Review applicants with metrics | Tap row |
170-| Application Review | `/(lister)/campaigns/[id]/applications/[appId]` | Approve/Reject | "Approve" / "Reject" |
171:| Submissions Inbox | `/(lister)/campaigns/[id]/submissions` | Review final videos | Tap row |
172-| Submission Review | `/(lister)/campaigns/[id]/submissions/[subId]` | Video + checklist + decide | "Approve" / "Reject" |
173-| Profile | `/(lister)/profile` | Company profile | "Edit" |
174:| Notifications | `/(lister)/notifications` | Activity inbox | Tap item |
175-
176-### 2.4 Shared / System
177-
178-| Screen | Route | Role | Purpose |
--
190-- Lister dashboard empty: "Post your first bounty."
191:- Applications inbox empty (lister): "Waiting for creators to apply."
192-- Network error: hard-shadow card with "Can't reach servers" + "Try again".
193-
194:**Age-bucketed nudges for non-expiring inboxes.** Applications and submissions do not auto-expire, so an inbox is never "full" in the OS sense — but stale rows rot quietly. On inboxes where any open row is older than a threshold, the list prepends a soft-warning banner (not an empty state — the list is non-empty):
195-
196:| Inbox | Trigger | Banner copy |
197-|---|---|---|
198:| Lister Applications Inbox | any pending application `created_at > 7 days` | *"N applications pending > 7 days. Review or close the listing."* |
199:| Lister Submissions Inbox | any pending submission `created_at > 5 days` | *"N submissions waiting > 5 days. Creators are watching the clock."* |
200-| Creator Applications | any pending application `created_at > 14 days` and no activity | *"N applications no one's touched in 2 weeks. Find a similar bounty?"* → routes to Feed |
201-
202-Banner is dismissible per-session but returns on next cold start until the condition clears.
203-
--
224-| Campaigns | megaphone | Campaigns | `/(lister)/campaigns` |
225:| Inbox | inbox | Inbox | `/(lister)/inbox` (apps + submissions, segmented) |
226-| Profile | building-2 | Profile | `/(lister)/profile` |
227-
228-Create-campaign is a **FAB** (cta color, hard shadow, 56px) on Dashboard + Campaigns tabs — not a center-tab bump.
229-
230:**Inbox model — relationship to §2.3 IA.** §2.3 lists Applications Inbox and Submissions Inbox as *campaign-scoped* routes (`/(lister)/campaigns/[id]/applications` and `/(lister)/campaigns/[id]/submissions`) because review always happens in-context for a specific campaign. The **Inbox tab** (`/(lister)/inbox`) is a cross-campaign roll-up — same underlying data, different entry point:
231-
232-- **Segmented header:** `Applications · Submissions` (segmented control, not two tabs — keeps the 4-tab bar).
233-- **Default segment:** whichever has the higher unreviewed count; ties go to Submissions (higher creator-anxiety cost per day of delay).
234-- **Row grouping:** grouped by campaign with `SectionHeader` rows; tapping a row pushes into the campaign-scoped review screen (same route as §2.3).
--
236-
237:So the campaign-scoped routes are the sources of truth; the Inbox tab is a lens. This matches how lister work actually happens — sometimes you think "that Nike campaign has applicants" (campaign-first), sometimes "do I have anything to review?" (inbox-first).
238-
239-### 3.3 Modal vs Stack vs Tab
240-
241-| Case | Pattern | Why |
--
262-| Submission approved/rejected | `marketify://submissions/:id` | Submission Detail with status change | Submitted tab → Submission Detail |
263:| Lister new application | `marketify://campaigns/:cid/applications/:aid` | Application Review | Home tab → Campaign Detail (`:cid`) → Applications Inbox → Application Review |
264:| Lister new submission | `marketify://campaigns/:cid/submissions/:sid` | Submission Review | Home tab → Campaign Detail (`:cid`) → Submissions Inbox → Submission Review |
265-| Metric refresh ready | `marketify://profile` | Profile with refresh banner | Profile tab (own root) |
266-
267:Built with `expo-router`. **Back-stack seeding rule:** deep links hydrate a realistic navigation spine, not just the target screen on top of the home tab. The table above spells out each link's spine — back-swipe from an Application Review lands the lister on the Applications Inbox they would have reached manually, not on Dashboard. One-level-deep links (creator Application Detail, Profile) seed a single parent tab and stop there.
268-
269----
270-
271-## 4. Key Screen Wireframes (text-described)
--
713-|---|---|---|
714:| **CampaignCard** | Feed + inbox cell | `title, company, platforms[], price, eligibility, variant: 'creator'\|'lister', counts?` |
715-| **StatusPill** | Status indicator | `status`, exposes `animateTo(newStatus)` worklet |
716-| **EligibilityBadge** | Compact badge on card | `eligible: boolean, reason?` |
717-| **EligibilityRow** | Single pre-condition row | `label, required, user_value, passed, action?` |
718-| **ConditionChecklist** | Post-condition list | `items[], mode: 'self'\|'review', onChange` |
--
737-| **TabBar** | Custom tab bar per role | `tabs[], activeIndex` |
738:| **NotificationRow** | Inbox item | `type, title, timestamp, read, href` |
739-| **ReuseBadge** | "Also submitted to N other campaigns" chip on submission row / review sheet — surfaces cross-posting without blocking it | `count, variant: 'inline'\|'header'` |
740-| **OverrideEligibilityDialog** | Approve-time confirmation for listers overriding a failed post-condition; renders the failed-conditions summary + required typed confirmation | `failedConditions[], onConfirm, onCancel` |
741-| **MetricStaleIndicator** | Per-metric tri-state marker attached to `MetricChip` (fresh / stale / refreshing / failed) — drives the visual in §5.4 | `state: 'fresh'\|'stale'\|'refreshing'\|'failed', retryAfterSec?, errorKind?: 'no_videos'\|'scrape_error', onRetry?` |
742-| **AddHandleSheet** | Bottom-sheet flow that adds a TikTok/IG handle inline from any surface (Campaign Detail eligibility row, Edit Handles). Owns its own state machine across `input → dispatching → scraping → (partial) → fresh \| failed` — see §4.2 | `platform: 'tiktok'\|'instagram', preFilledHandle?: string, onSuccess: (socialLinkId) => void, onCancel: () => void` |
