230:**Inbox model — relationship to §2.3 IA.** §2.3 lists Applications Inbox and Submissions Inbox as *campaign-scoped* routes (`/(lister)/campaigns/[id]/applications` and `/(lister)/campaigns/[id]/submissions`) because review always happens in-context for a specific campaign. The **Inbox tab** (`/(lister)/inbox`) is a cross-campaign roll-up — same underlying data, different entry point:
231-
232-- **Segmented header:** `Applications · Submissions` (segmented control, not two tabs — keeps the 4-tab bar).
233-- **Default segment:** whichever has the higher unreviewed count; ties go to Submissions (higher creator-anxiety cost per day of delay).
234-- **Row grouping:** grouped by campaign with `SectionHeader` rows; tapping a row pushes into the campaign-scoped review screen (same route as §2.3).
--
236-
237:So the campaign-scoped routes are the sources of truth; the Inbox tab is a lens. This matches how lister work actually happens — sometimes you think "that Nike campaign has applicants" (campaign-first), sometimes "do I have anything to review?" (inbox-first).
238-
239-### 3.3 Modal vs Stack vs Tab
240-
241-| Case | Pattern | Why |
--
262-| Submission approved/rejected | `marketify://submissions/:id` | Submission Detail with status change | Submitted tab → Submission Detail |
263:| Lister new application | `marketify://campaigns/:cid/applications/:aid` | Application Review | Home tab → Campaign Detail (`:cid`) → Applications Inbox → Application Review |
264:| Lister new submission | `marketify://campaigns/:cid/submissions/:sid` | Submission Review | Home tab → Campaign Detail (`:cid`) → Submissions Inbox → Submission Review |
265-| Metric refresh ready | `marketify://profile` | Profile with refresh banner | Profile tab (own root) |
266-
267:Built with `expo-router`. **Back-stack seeding rule:** deep links hydrate a realistic navigation spine, not just the target screen on top of the home tab. The table above spells out each link's spine — back-swipe from an Application Review lands the lister on the Applications Inbox they would have reached manually, not on Dashboard. One-level-deep links (creator Application Detail, Profile) seed a single parent tab and stop there.
268-
269----
270-
271-## 4. Key Screen Wireframes (text-described)
--
713-|---|---|---|
714:| **CampaignCard** | Feed + inbox cell | `title, company, platforms[], price, eligibility, variant: 'creator'\|'lister', counts?` |
715-| **StatusPill** | Status indicator | `status`, exposes `animateTo(newStatus)` worklet |
716-| **EligibilityBadge** | Compact badge on card | `eligible: boolean, reason?` |
717-| **EligibilityRow** | Single pre-condition row | `label, required, user_value, passed, action?` |
718-| **ConditionChecklist** | Post-condition list | `items[], mode: 'self'\|'review', onChange` |
--
737-| **TabBar** | Custom tab bar per role | `tabs[], activeIndex` |
738:| **NotificationRow** | Inbox item | `type, title, timestamp, read, href` |
739-| **ReuseBadge** | "Also submitted to N other campaigns" chip on submission row / review sheet — surfaces cross-posting without blocking it | `count, variant: 'inline'\|'header'` |
740-| **OverrideEligibilityDialog** | Approve-time confirmation for listers overriding a failed post-condition; renders the failed-conditions summary + required typed confirmation | `failedConditions[], onConfirm, onCancel` |
741-| **MetricStaleIndicator** | Per-metric tri-state marker attached to `MetricChip` (fresh / stale / refreshing / failed) — drives the visual in §5.4 | `state: 'fresh'\|'stale'\|'refreshing'\|'failed', retryAfterSec?, errorKind?: 'no_videos'\|'scrape_error', onRetry?` |
742-| **AddHandleSheet** | Bottom-sheet flow that adds a TikTok/IG handle inline from any surface (Campaign Detail eligibility row, Edit Handles). Owns its own state machine across `input → dispatching → scraping → (partial) → fresh \| failed` — see §4.2 | `platform: 'tiktok'\|'instagram', preFilledHandle?: string, onSuccess: (socialLinkId) => void, onCancel: () => void` |

input.query:
-- US-056 — Lister Inbox: Applications tab RPC.
--
-- Contract: `public.list_my_applications_as_lister()` returns every
-- application (status in pending/approved/rejected) targeting a listing
-- owned by the caller (`listings.lister_id = public.current_user_id()`),
-- joined to the creator's username + tiktok/instagram handles +
-- denormalized follower/view metrics. One row per application.
--
-- Why SECURITY DEFINER + explicit ownership check (Codebase Pattern #118,
-- #117): a plain PostgREST embed on `applications` would need to reach
-- `users.username` (users_self_select RLS is self-only → silent null),
-- `social_links.handle` (social_links_lister_read RLS only shows rows
-- whose user has applied to the caller's listings — works for this
-- screen but is fragile to future tightening), and
-- `creator_profiles.*_follower_count` (creator_profiles_lister_read
-- analogous). DEFINER plus an explicit lister_id gate collapses all
-- four RLS checks into one authoritative ownership filter, and matches
-- the `list_my_applications` (US-043) / `list_my_campaigns` (US-054)
-- precedent shape.
--
-- Status filter: AC scopes the inbox to Pending / Approved / Rejected.
-- Three enum values — `withdrawn`, `cancelled_listing_edit`,
-- `cancelled_listing_closed` — are intentionally excluded from this
-- inbox because they represent creator-initiated or cascade-driven
-- cancellations that do not require lister review. They remain visible
-- on the campaign-scoped application detail screens (US-057+).
--
-- Social-link join semantics: filter by `status <> 'unlinked'` so rows
-- with a subsequently removed handle (history-preserved per US-012
-- design) are omitted. The partial unique index
-- `social_links_active_uniq (user_id, platform) WHERE status <> 'unlinked'`
-- guarantees at most one active link per (user, platform), so the
-- left-join cannot duplicate the applications row. When a creator has
-- not linked a platform at all, the handle/metrics columns come back
-- NULL and the UI surfaces the "no handle" state inline.
--
-- Ordering: `created_at desc` — freshest applications first. This
-- matches `list_my_applications` (US-043) and the inbox UX spec
-- (docs/design.md §3.2: "grouped by campaign" — the UI is responsible
-- for SectionHeader grouping; the RPC provides the data in recency
-- order so the intra-campaign cells remain recency-ordered).
--
-- Codebase Patterns referenced:
--   #117 Audit PostgREST embeds against target-table RLS
--   #118 SECURITY DEFINER + ownership-check list-my-rows skeleton
--   #119 `list_my_<things>` naming convention; argumentless; public schema
--   #42  `set search_path = ''` + schema-qualify every user-defined ref

create or replace function public.list_my_applications_as_lister()
returns table (
  application_id              uuid,
  status                      public.application_status,
  created_at                  timestamptz,
  cover_note                  text,
  listing_id                  uuid,
  listing_title               text,
  creator_user_id             uuid,
  creator_username            text,
  tiktok_handle               text,
  tiktok_follower_count       integer,
  instagram_handle            text,
  instagram_follower_count    integer,
  instagram_avg_views_last_10 integer
)
language sql
stable
security definer
set search_path = ''
as $$
  select a.id                              as application_id,
         a.status,
         a.created_at,
         a.cover_note,
         a.listing_id,
         l.title                           as listing_title,
         a.creator_id                      as creator_user_id,
         u.username::text                  as creator_username,
         tt.handle::text                   as tiktok_handle,
         cp.tiktok_follower_count,
         ig.handle::text                   as instagram_handle,
         cp.instagram_follower_count,
         cp.instagram_avg_views_last_10
    from public.applications a
    join public.listings l
      on l.id = a.listing_id
    left join public.users u
      on u.id = a.creator_id
    left join public.creator_profiles cp
      on cp.user_id = a.creator_id
    left join public.social_links tt
      on tt.user_id = a.creator_id
     and tt.platform = 'tiktok'::public.platform
     and tt.status   <> 'unlinked'::public.social_link_status
    left join public.social_links ig
      on ig.user_id = a.creator_id
     and ig.platform = 'instagram'::public.platform
     and ig.status   <> 'unlinked'::public.social_link_status
   where l.lister_id = public.current_user_id()
     and a.status in (
       'pending'::public.application_status,
       'approved'::public.application_status,
       'rejected'::public.application_status
     )
   order by a.created_at desc;
$$;

revoke all on function public.list_my_applications_as_lister() from public;
revoke all on function public.list_my_applications_as_lister() from anon;
grant execute on function public.list_my_applications_as_lister() to authenticated;

-- precedent shape.

-- Three enum values — `withdrawn`, `cancelled_listing_edit`,
-- `cancelled_listing_closed` — are intentionally excluded from this
-- inbox because they represent creator-initiated or cascade-driven
-- cancellations that do not require lister review. They remain visible
-- on the campaign-scoped application detail screens (US-057+).

-- with a subsequently removed handle (history-preserved per US-012
-- design) are omitted. The partial unique index

-- guarantees at most one active link per (user, platform), so the
-- left-join cannot duplicate the applications row. When a creator has
-- not linked a platform at all, the handle/metrics columns come back
-- NULL and the UI surfaces the "no handle" state inline.

-- matches `list_my_applications` (US-043) and the inbox UX spec
-- (docs/design.md §3.2: "grouped by campaign" — the UI is responsible
-- for SectionHeader grouping; the RPC provides the data in recency

language sql
stable
security definer
set search_path = ''
as $$
  select a.id                              as application_id,
         a.status,
         a.created_at,
         a.cover_note,
         a.listing_id,
         l.title                           as listing_title,
         a.creator_id                      as creator_user_id,
         u.username::text                  as creator_username,
         tt.handle::text                   as tiktok_handle,

--   #42  `set search_path = ''` + schema-qualify every user-defined ref

  status                      public.application_status,

      on u.id = a.creator_id
    left join public.creator_profiles cp
      on cp.user_id = a.creator_id
    left join public.social_links tt
      on tt.user_id = a.creator_id
     and tt.platform = 'tiktok'::public.platform
     and tt.status   <> 'unlinked'::public.social_link_status
    left join public.social_links ig
      on ig.user_id = a.creator_id
     and ig.platform = 'instagram'::public.platform
     and ig.status   <> 'unlinked'::public.social_link_status
   where l.lister_id = public.current_user_id()
     and a.status in (
       'pending'::public.application_status,
       'approved'::public.application_status,
       'rejected'::public.application_status
     )
   order by a.created_at desc;
$$;

message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   Execute one iteration of the Ralph autonomous coding loop for Marketify (Expo + React Native + Supabase marketplace MVP). Per `scripts/ralph/CLAUDE.md` instructions: orient via `prd.json` + `progress.txt`, pick the lowest-priority story with `passes: false`, implement it surgically, run quality gates (tsc + lint + supabase advisors), mobile-mcp verify any UI, pass through the `feature-dev:code-reviewer` subagent, commit, and update the ledger. Each iteration ships exactly one story.

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Ralph autonomous loop (stateless iteration pattern; decisions persisted in git + prd.json + progress.txt)
   - Expo Router v6 (file-based routing, route groups, `<Slot />` under Expo Go + SDK 54 new-arch)
   - Supabase MCP (apply_migration, execute_sql, generate_typescript_types, get_advisors, get_logs)
   - PostgreSQL SECURITY DEFINER RPC pattern with explicit ownership check (`current_user_id()` helper, `set search_path = ''`)
   - RLS silent-null-embed trap (Codebase Pattern #117) — why we prefer DEFINER RPCs over PostgREST embeds for cross-tenant reads
   - Supabase Realtime tenant hibernation (Pattern #124 — postgres_changes replication slot never starts, only broadcast `_messages_` slot)
   - Mobile-mcp iOS simulator verification with DevRolePicker (dev-only JWT mint for seeded roles)
   - Two-phase verification probe pattern (Pattern #122) for screens whose populated case isn't reachable from DevRolePicker
   - Enum-to-UI-bucket maps typed as `Record<DBEnum, UISegment>` for compile-time exhaustiveness (Pattern #121)
   - Custom neubrutalist TabBar (Pattern #95 — native `<Tabs>` crashes in Expo Go)

3. Files and Code Sections:
   - `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/scripts/ralph/prd.json` (977 lines)
     - Edited US-044 notes to append 4th re-verify log entry documenting continuing realtime tenant breakage.
     - Contains 65 user stories; currently 6 unfinished.
   
   - `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/scripts/ralph/progress.txt` (1200 lines)
     - Contains 138+ Codebase Patterns section at the top; most-relevant patterns for US-056: #117 (RLS embed audit), #118 (list-my-rows RPC skeleton), #119 (naming convention), #121 (enum→bucket maps), #122 (two-phase probe), #123 (realtime child-of-join no-filter), #124 (realtime tenant decision rule), #42 (trigger/function hardening).
   
   - `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/supabase/migrations/20260418130000_us_056_list_my_applications_as_lister_rpc.sql` (CREATED)
     - SECURITY DEFINER SQL function returning flat joined shape (application + listing + creator username + tiktok/ig handles + metrics).
     - Filters `status in ('pending','approved','rejected')` — excludes withdrawn/cancelled_listing_edit/cancelled_listing_closed by design.
     - Left joins to `users`, `creator_profiles`, and TWO `social_links` joins (tiktok + instagram, filtered `status <> 'unlinked'`).
     - `where l.lister_id = public.current_user_id()` as the ownership gate.
     - Revoke from public/anon; grant execute to authenticated.
     - Applied via Supabase MCP and verified via simulated-JWT SQL query returning 6 rows for seed lister.
   
   - `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/src/types/supabase.ts` (NOT YET WRITTEN — types regenerated but pending save)
     - Regenerated types now include:
       ```
       list_my_applications_as_lister: {
         Args: never

           application_id: string

           tiktok_handle: string

       }
       ```
   
   - `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/app/(lister)/inbox.tsx` (PENDING DELETION)
     - Current: 12-line placeholder using `<TabPlaceholder>`. Will be deleted and replaced with `inbox/` directory.
   
   - `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/app/(lister)/inbox/_layout.tsx` (PENDING CREATION)
     - Will render `<Slot />`. Parent Applications/Submissions segmented control deferred to US-058.
   
   - `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/app/(lister)/inbox/index.tsx` (PENDING CREATION)
     - Will render `<Redirect href="/(lister)/inbox/applications" />` so the TabBar's `/(lister)/inbox` navigation lands on Applications by default.
   
   - `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/app/(lister)/inbox/applications.tsx` (PENDING CREATION — the story's primary deliverable)
     - Will mirror `app/(creator)/applications.tsx` structure (read during this session).
     - 3 segments: Pending / Approved / Rejected (not 4 like creator side — cancelled out of scope).
     - Calls `supabase.rpc('list_my_applications_as_lister')`.
     - Row card: campaign title as SectionHeader, creator `@username`, TikTok + IG handles (mono font), follower counts, IG avg views, pitch preview from `cover_note`, StatusPill.

file.content:
-- US-056 — Lister Inbox: Applications tab RPC.
--
-- Contract: `public.list_my_applications_as_lister()` returns every
-- application (status in pending/approved/rejected) targeting a listing
-- owned by the caller (`listings.lister_id = public.current_user_id()`),
-- joined to the creator's username + tiktok/instagram handles +
-- denormalized follower/view metrics. One row per application.
--
-- Why SECURITY DEFINER + explicit ownership check (Codebase Pattern #118,
-- #117): a plain PostgREST embed on `applications` would need to reach
-- `users.username` (users_self_select RLS is self-only → silent null),
-- `social_links.handle` (social_links_lister_read RLS only shows rows
-- whose user has applied to the caller's listings — works for this
-- screen but is fragile to future tightening), and
-- `creator_profiles.*_follower_count` (creator_profiles_lister_read
-- analogous). DEFINER plus an explicit lister_id gate collapses all
-- four RLS checks into one authoritative ownership filter, and matches
-- the `list_my_applications` (US-043) / `list_my_campaigns` (US-054)
-- precedent shape.
--
-- Status filter: AC scopes the inbox to Pending / Approved / Rejected.
-- Three enum values — `withdrawn`, `cancelled_listing_edit`,
-- `cancelled_listing_closed` — are intentionally excluded from this
-- inbox because they represent creator-initiated or cascade-driven
-- cancellations that do not require lister review. They remain visible
-- on the campaign-scoped application detail screens (US-057+).
--
-- Social-link join semantics: filter by `status <> 'unlinked'` so rows
-- with a subsequently removed handle (history-preserved per US-012
-- design) are omitted. The partial unique index
-- `social_links_active_uniq (user_id, platform) WHERE status <> 'unlinked'`
-- guarantees at most one active link per (user, platform), so the
-- left-join cannot duplicate the applications row. When a creator has
-- not linked a platform at all, the handle/metrics columns come back
-- NULL and the UI surfaces the "no handle" state inline.
--
-- Ordering: `created_at desc` — freshest applications first. This
-- matches `list_my_applications` (US-043) and the inbox UX spec
-- (docs/design.md §3.2: "grouped by campaign" — the UI is responsible
-- for SectionHeader grouping; the RPC provides the data in recency
-- order so the intra-campaign cells remain recency-ordered).
--
-- Codebase Patterns referenced:
--   #117 Audit PostgREST embeds against target-table RLS
--   #118 SECURITY DEFINER + ownership-check list-my-rows skeleton
--   #119 `list_my_<things>` naming convention; argumentless; public schema
--   #42  `set search_path = ''` + schema-qualify every user-defined ref

create or replace function public.list_my_applications_as_lister()
returns table (
  application_id              uuid,
  status                      public.application_status,
  created_at                  timestamptz,
  cover_note                  text,
  listing_id                  uuid,
  listing_title               text,
  creator_user_id             uuid,
  creator_username            text,
  tiktok_handle               text,
  tiktok_follower_count       integer,
  instagram_handle            text,
  instagram_follower_count    integer,
  instagram_avg_views_last_10 integer
)
language sql
stable
security definer
set search_path = ''
as $$
  select a.id                              as application_id,
         a.status,
         a.created_at,
         a.cover_note,
         a.listing_id,
         l.title                           as listing_title,
         a.creator_id                      as creator_user_id,
         u.username::text                  as creator_username,
         tt.handle::text                   as tiktok_handle,
         cp.tiktok_follower_count,
         ig.handle::text                   as instagram_handle,
         cp.instagram_follower_count,
         cp.instagram_avg_views_last_10
    from public.applications a
    join public.listings l
      on l.id = a.listing_id
    left join public.users u
      on u.id = a.creator_id
    left join public.creator_profiles cp
      on cp.user_id = a.creator_id
    left join public.social_links tt
      on tt.user_id = a.creator_id
     and tt.platform = 'tiktok'::public.platform
     and tt.status   <> 'unlinked'::public.social_link_status
    left join public.social_links ig
      on ig.user_id = a.creator_id
     and ig.platform = 'instagram'::public.platform
     and ig.status   <> 'unlinked'::public.social_link_status
   where l.lister_id = public.current_user_id()
     and a.status in (
       'pending'::public.application_status,
       'approved'::public.application_status,
       'rejected'::public.application_status
     )
   order by a.created_at desc;
$$;

revoke all on function public.list_my_applications_as_lister() from public;
revoke all on function public.list_my_applications_as_lister() from anon;
grant execute on function public.list_my_applications_as_lister() to authenticated;

# Marketify — Ralph Agent Instructions

You are an autonomous coding agent building **Marketify**, a mobile marketplace where creators claim bounties from listers (Expo + React Native + Supabase). Each invocation is a fresh Claude Code instance with no prior memory. Every persistent decision lives in git history, `scripts/ralph/progress.txt`, and `scripts/ralph/prd.json`.

---

## Authoritative specs (read these for any ambiguity)

| File | What it defines |
|---|---|
| `docs/product-plan.md` | Canonical product, 12-step build sequence, scope cuts |
| `docs/design.md` | Visual language, component inventory, screen catalog, motion |
| `docs/tech-architecture.md` | DB schema (§4.7), RLS, triggers, Apify integration (§3), edge-function spec, eligibility engine |

If a story is ambiguous, the specs win. Do **not** invent product behavior the docs don't sanction. If something is genuinely missing, append a `Spec gap:` note to `progress.txt` and pick the simplest interpretation that aligns with the docs.

---

## The loop you run, every iteration

1. **Orient.** Read `scripts/ralph/prd.json` and `scripts/ralph/progress.txt`. Pay special attention to the `## Codebase Patterns` section at the top of `progress.txt` — it consolidates everything previous iterations learned.
2. **Branch.** Make sure you're on the branch named in `prd.json.branchName`. If it doesn't exist, create it from `main`.
3. **Pick one story.** Lowest `priority` number where `passes: false`. Work on **exactly one** story per iteration.
4. **Research before coding.** If understanding the story requires reading >3 files, **delegate to the Explore subagent** (`subagent_type: "Explore"`) so your own context stays clean for implementation. Pass it the story plus pointers into the docs.
5. **Implement.** Make the minimal change set that satisfies every acceptance criterion. Match existing conventions. Don't refactor adjacent code.
6. **Quality gates** (run in this order, stop on first failure and fix before continuing):
   - `npx tsc --noEmit` — must pass with zero errors
   - `npx expo lint` — must pass (warnings allowed only if pre-existing in main)
   - Migration sanity: any new SQL must apply cleanly via Supabase MCP without manual fix-up
7. **Code review (mandatory before commit).** Spawn the `feature-dev:code-reviewer` subagent with the diff scope, the story acceptance criteria, and the relevant doc sections. Address every issue rated medium or higher. Re-run quality gates after fixes.
8. **Mobile verification (mandatory for any UI story).** Use `mobile-mcp` to:
   - Launch the app on the iOS simulator (`mcp__mobile-mcp__mobile_launch_app`)
   - Navigate to the screen the story touches
   - Exercise the golden path
   - Take a screenshot (`mcp__mobile-mcp__mobile_take_screenshot`) and reference it in the progress note
   - If the simulator is unavailable, **do not silently skip** — set `passes: false`, write a `notes` entry on the story explaining the blocker, and move to the next story.
9. **Commit.** Stage everything you changed (no `git add -A` blanket — list paths). Commit message format:
   ```
   feat: [US-XXX] Story title
   ```
10. **Mark done.** In `prd.json`, set `passes: true` for the story you just finished. Append a dated entry to `progress.txt` (format below).
11. **Stop check.** If every story has `passes: true`, your final response must contain `<promise>COMPLETE</promise>` and nothing else useful. Otherwise end normally — the next iteration picks up.

---

## MCP servers — use these instead of CLI wherever possible

### Supabase (`mcp__plugin_supabase_supabase__*`) — required for ALL database work
- `apply_migration` — every schema change. Migrations are append-only and named after the story (e.g. `us_006_metric_snapshots_denorm_trigger`).
- `execute_sql` — read-only queries, RLS verification, seed data inspection.
- `list_tables`, `list_migrations`, `list_extensions` — orient before changing things.
- `generate_typescript_types` — regenerate `src/types/supabase.ts` after every migration. Commit the regenerated file with the migration.
- `deploy_edge_function`, `get_edge_function`, `list_edge_functions` — for `apify-core`, `auth-*`, `metrics-refresh`, the webhook receiver, etc.
- `get_advisors` — run with `type: "security"` after any schema or RLS change. Address every error-level advisor before committing.
- `get_logs` — when an edge function or query misbehaves.

Do **not** use the local `supabase` CLI for migrations or queries. The MCP is the source of truth so iterations stay consistent.

### Mobile (`mcp__mobile-mcp__*`) — required for any UI verification
- `mobile_list_available_devices` then `mobile_launch_app` to boot the app.
- `mobile_list_elements_on_screen` + `mobile_click_on_screen_at_coordinates` to drive flows.
- `mobile_take_screenshot` after each meaningful state — name screenshots `us-XXX-state.png` and store under `scripts/ralph/screenshots/`.
- Use `mobile_type_keys` rather than tapping virtual keys when filling text inputs.

### Context7 (`mcp__plugin_context7_context7__*`) — for any library docs
Use **before** writing non-trivial integration code with Expo, Reanimated, NativeWind, Supabase JS, React Navigation, etc. Your training data may be stale. `resolve-library-id` then `query-docs`.

### Apify
The API key is in `.env` as `APIFY_KEY`. Always read it at runtime via `process.env.APIFY_KEY` (or `Deno.env.get("APIFY_KEY")` inside edge functions). **Never** paste the key into source. Actor catalog and input schemas live in `docs/tech-architecture.md` §3.

---

## Subagent delegation — preserve your context

You will burn out your own context window if you try to do everything in-line. Delegate aggressively:

| When | Subagent | Why |
|---|---|---|
| Need to read >3 files to understand the codebase | `Explore` | Returns a focused summary instead of dumping raw file contents into your context |
| Before every commit | `feature-dev:code-reviewer` | Independent review against acceptance criteria + doc sections |
| Implementation will touch >5 files (e.g. wiring a new edge function end-to-end) | `feature-dev:code-architect` first, then implement yourself | Plan once, execute once |
| Need to research how something works before changing it | `feature-dev:code-explorer` | Maps the existing layers without polluting your context |
| Open-ended search across the repo | `general-purpose` | Iterates until it finds the answer |

When briefing a subagent, give it the story ID, acceptance criteria, and a pointer to the relevant `docs/*.md` section. Do **not** tell it "based on your findings, implement" — synthesize the result yourself.

---

## Project quality conventions (these become permanent — additions go in `progress.txt` Codebase Patterns)

- **TypeScript everywhere.** No `any` without a `// eslint-disable-next-line` and a comment explaining why. Generated Supabase types are imported from `src/types/supabase.ts`.
- **Migrations are append-only.** Never edit a previously applied migration. If you need to change something, write a new migration that alters the prior state.
- **Edge functions** live in `supabase/functions/<name>/index.ts`. Each one has a one-paragraph header comment naming the contract (request shape → response shape) and the auth requirement.
- **RLS is mandatory** on every table that holds user data. After applying any RLS migration, run `get_advisors({type: "security"})` and fix every finding before committing.
- **Component organization:** primitives in `src/components/primitives/`, screen-specific composites in `src/screens/<screen>/`, shared composites in `src/components/shared/`.
- **No emoji** anywhere in the product UI (per `docs/design.md` — Lucide icons only).
- **No payments code** in v1 — `price_cents` is display-only.
- **Dual-role accounts** are out of scope for v1. One role per account.

---

## Progress log format (`progress.txt`)

Append (never replace) using this format:

```
## YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM — US-XXX
- What was implemented (1–3 bullets, concrete)
- Files changed (bulleted list)
- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors ✓ / mobile-mcp ✓
- Code-reviewer verdict: [approved | issues fixed in this commit]
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - Patterns discovered
  - Gotchas
  - Useful pointers
---
```

If a learning is **general and reusable across stories**, also promote it into the `## Codebase Patterns` section at the top of `progress.txt`. That section is what every future iteration leans on first — keep it tight, deduplicated, and signal-only.

---

## Stop condition

After updating `prd.json`, count remaining `passes: false` stories. If zero, your last line in this response must be exactly:

```
<promise>COMPLETE</promise>
```

Otherwise end normally and let the loop spawn the next iteration.

---

## Hard rules (do not violate)

- **One story per iteration.** Don't peek ahead.
- **Never commit broken code.** A failing typecheck means you don't commit, you don't mark `passes: true`, you write a `notes` entry, and the next iteration retries.
- **Never skip the code-reviewer subagent.** It is the second pair of eyes that keeps quality from drifting across iterations.
- **Never skip mobile verification on UI stories.** A screen "looking right in the JSX" is not the same as it actually rendering correctly.
- **Never read `.env` contents into the conversation.** Reference variable names only.
- **Never edit `docs/product-plan.md`, `docs/design.md`, or `docs/tech-architecture.md`** unless a story explicitly says to. They are specs, not work product.
- **Never invent stories** or add new ones to `prd.json` mid-loop. If you discover a missing piece, append it as a `Spec gap:` line in `progress.txt` so the human can decide.

## plugin:context7:context7
Use this server to fetch current documentation whenever the user asks about a library, framework, SDK, API, CLI tool, or cloud service -- even well-known ones like React, Next.js, Prisma, Express, Tailwind, Django, or Spring Boot. This includes API syntax, configuration, version migration, library-specific debugging, setup instructions, and CLI tool usage. Use even when you think you know the answer -- your training data may not reflect recent changes. Prefer this over web search for library docs.

Do not use for: refactoring, writing scripts from scratch, debugging business logic, code review, or general programming concepts.

<EXTREMELY_IMPORTANT>
You have superpowers.

**Below is the full content of your 'superpowers:using-superpowers' skill - your introduction to using skills. For all other skills, use the 'Skill' tool:**

---
name: using-superpowers
description: Use when starting any conversation - establishes how to find and use skills, requiring Skill tool invocation before ANY response including clarifying questions
---

<SUBAGENT-STOP>
If you were dispatched as a subagent to execute a specific task, skip this skill.
</SUBAGENT-STOP>

<EXTREMELY-IMPORTANT>
If you think there is even a 1% chance a skill might apply to what you are doing, you ABSOLUTELY MUST invoke the skill.

IF A SKILL APPLIES TO YOUR TASK, YOU DO NOT HAVE A CHOICE. YOU MUST USE IT.

This is not negotiable. This is not optional. You cannot rationalize your way out of this.
</EXTREMELY-IMPORTANT>

## Instruction Priority

Superpowers skills override default system prompt behavior, but **user instructions always take precedence**:

1. **User's explicit instructions** (CLAUDE.md, GEMINI.md, AGENTS.md, direct requests) — highest priority
2. **Superpowers skills** — override default system behavior where they conflict
3. **Default system prompt** — lowest priority

If CLAUDE.md, GEMINI.md, or AGENTS.md says "don't use TDD" and a skill says "always use TDD," follow the user's instructions. The user is in control.

## How to Access Skills

**In Claude Code:** Use the `Skill` tool. When you invoke a skill, its content is loaded and presented to you—follow it directly. Never use the Read tool on skill files.

**In Copilot CLI:** Use the `skill` tool. Skills are auto-discovered from installed plugins. The `skill` tool works the same as Claude Code's `Skill` tool.

**In Gemini CLI:** Skills activate via the `activate_skill` tool. Gemini loads skill metadata at session start and activates the full content on demand.

**In other environments:** Check your platform's documentation for how skills are loaded.

## Platform Adaptation

Skills use Claude Code tool names. Non-CC platforms: see `references/copilot-tools.md` (Copilot CLI), `references/codex-tools.md` (Codex) for tool equivalents. Gemini CLI users get the tool mapping loaded automatically via GEMINI.md.

# Using Skills

## The Rule

**Invoke relevant or requested skills BEFORE any response or action.** Even a 1% chance a skill might apply means that you should invoke the skill to check. If an invoked skill turns out to be wrong for the situation, you don't need to use it.

```dot
digraph skill_flow {
    "User message received" [shape=doublecircle];
    "About to EnterPlanMode?" [shape=doublecircle];
    "Already brainstormed?" [shape=diamond];
    "Invoke brainstorming skill" [shape=box];
    "Might any skill apply?" [shape=diamond];
    "Invoke Skill tool" [shape=box];
    "Announce: 'Using [skill] to [purpose]'" [shape=box];
    "Has checklist?" [shape=diamond];
    "Create TodoWrite todo per item" [shape=box];
    "Follow skill exactly" [shape=box];
    "Respond (including clarifications)" [shape=doublecircle];

    "About to EnterPlanMode?" -> "Already brainstormed?";
    "Already brainstormed?" -> "Invoke brainstorming skill" [label="no"];
    "Already brainstormed?" -> "Might any skill apply?" [label="yes"];
    "Invoke brainstorming skill" -> "Might any skill apply?";

    "User message received" -> "Might any skill apply?";
    "Might any skill apply?" -> "Invoke Skill tool" [label="yes, even 1%"];
    "Might any skill apply?" -> "Respond (including clarifications)" [label="definitely not"];
    "Invoke Skill tool" -> "Announce: 'Using [skill] to [purpose]'";
    "Announce: 'Using [skill] to [purpose]'" -> "Has checklist?";
    "Has checklist?" -> "Create TodoWrite todo per item" [label="yes"];
    "Has checklist?" -> "Follow skill exactly" [label="no"];
    "Create TodoWrite todo per item" -> "Follow skill exactly";
}
```

## Red Flags

These thoughts mean STOP—you're rationalizing:

| Thought | Reality |
|---------|---------|
| "This is just a simple question" | Questions are tasks. Check for skills. |
| "I need more context first" | Skill check comes BEFORE clarifying questions. |
| "Let me explore the codebase first" | Skills tell you HOW to explore. Check first. |
| "I can check git/files quickly" | Files lack conversation context. Check for skills. |
| "Let me gather information first" | Skills tell you HOW to gather information. |
| "This doesn't need a formal skill" | If a skill exists, use it. |
| "I remember this skill" | Skills evolve. Read current version. |
| "This doesn't count as a task" | Action = task. Check for skills. |
| "The skill is overkill" | Simple things become complex. Use it. |
| "I'll just do this one thing first" | Check BEFORE doing anything. |
| "This feels productive" | Undisciplined action wastes time. Skills prevent this. |
| "I know what that means" | Knowing the concept ≠ using the skill. Invoke it. |

## Skill Priority

When multiple skills could apply, use this order:

1. **Process skills first** (brainstorming, debugging) - these determine HOW to approach the task
2. **Implementation skills second** (frontend-design, mcp-builder) - these guide execution

"Let's build X" → brainstorming first, then implementation skills.
"Fix this bug" → debugging first, then domain-specific skills.

## Skill Types

**Rigid** (TDD, debugging): Follow exactly. Don't adapt away discipline.

**Flexible** (patterns): Adapt principles to context.

The skill itself tells you which.

## User Instructions

Instructions say WHAT, not HOW. "Add X" or "Fix Y" doesn't mean skip workflows.


</EXTREMELY_IMPORTANT>

content.content: Todos have been modified successfully. Ensure that you continue to use the todo list to track your progress. Please proceed with the current tasks if applicable

1	import { Redirect, Slot, usePathname } from 'expo-router';
2	import { Building2, Inbox, LayoutDashboard, Megaphone } from 'lucide-react-native';
3	import { StyleSheet, View } from 'react-native';
4	import { TabBar, type TabDef } from '@/components/shared/TabBar';
5	import { colors } from '@/design/tokens';
6	import { useAuth } from '@/lib/auth';
7	
8	// Lister tab group — Home (index) / Campaigns / Inbox / Profile per US-031 AC
9	// + docs/design.md §3.2. Same custom-TabBar rationale as the creator layout:
10	// expo-router's native `<Tabs>` crashes in Expo Go + SDK 54 new-arch. The FAB
11	// is mounted on the Home screen per §3.2 ("Create-campaign is a FAB on
12	// Dashboard + Campaigns tabs — not a center-tab bump").
13	const LISTER_TABS: TabDef[] = [
14	  {
15	    label: 'Home',
16	    icon: LayoutDashboard,
17	    path: '/(lister)/dashboard',
18	    activePathname: '/dashboard',
19	    testID: 'tab-dashboard',
20	  },
21	  {
22	    label: 'Campaigns',
23	    icon: Megaphone,
24	    path: '/(lister)/campaigns',
25	    activePathname: '/campaigns',
26	    testID: 'tab-campaigns',
27	  },
28	  {
29	    label: 'Inbox',
30	    icon: Inbox,
31	    path: '/(lister)/inbox',
32	    activePathname: '/inbox',
33	    testID: 'tab-inbox',
34	  },
35	  {
36	    label: 'Profile',
37	    icon: Building2,
38	    path: '/(lister)/profile',
39	    activePathname: '/profile',
40	    testID: 'tab-profile',
41	  },
42	];
43	
44	export default function ListerLayout() {
45	  const { role } = useAuth();
46	  const pathname = usePathname();
47	  if (role !== 'lister') return <Redirect href="/(auth)" />;
48	
49	  // Hide the tab bar when the user is inside the campaign-creation wizard
50	  // (US-049+) or the edit-campaign screen (US-055). Route-group parens are
51	  // stripped from the URL, so `/(lister)/campaigns/new/step-1` appears
52	  // here as `/campaigns/new/step-1`, and `/(lister)/campaigns/[id]/edit`
53	  // appears as `/campaigns/<uuid>/edit`.
54	  const hideTabBar =
55	    pathname.startsWith('/campaigns/new') || /^\/campaigns\/[^/]+\/edit$/.test(pathname);
56	
57	  return (
58	    <View style={styles.root}>
59	      <View style={styles.content}>
60	        <Slot />
61	      </View>
62	      {hideTabBar ? null : <TabBar tabs={LISTER_TABS} />}
63	    </View>
64	  );
65	}
66	
67	const styles = StyleSheet.create({
68	  root: { flex: 1, backgroundColor: colors.canvas },
69	  content: { flex: 1 },
70	});
71

232-- **Segmented header:** `Applications · Submissions` (segmented control, not two tabs — keeps the 4-tab bar).

235-- **Empty state:** if both segments empty, "Nothing to review. Good time to post another bounty." with a CTA button to Create Campaign.

file.content:
-- US-044 — add public.applications to the supabase_realtime publication so
-- creators receive UPDATE events on their own applications (RLS applied by
-- the Realtime broker via supabase.realtime.setAuth(marketify_jwt)). The
-- default replica identity (primary key only) is sufficient because the
-- client only reads payload.new.id + payload.new.status; the old-row
-- record is not consulted.

alter publication supabase_realtime add table public.applications;

input.prompt:
Review the US-056 implementation: Marketify's Lister Inbox Applications tab.

## Story acceptance criteria (from scripts/ralph/prd.json US-056)
- `app/(lister)/inbox/applications.tsx` queries applications joined to listings + creator_profiles
- Each row shows creator handles (mono), TikTok followers, IG followers + avg views, application pitch preview
- Realtime subscription for inserts
- Mobile-mcp verification done (empty states) + SQL-level populated probe (6 rows seen under seed lister's simulated JWT)

## Files to review
1. `supabase/migrations/20260418130000_us_056_list_my_applications_as_lister_rpc.sql` — SECURITY DEFINER RPC. Filters `status in (pending,approved,rejected)`, left-joins users/creator_profiles/social_links (tt + ig), gates by `listings.lister_id = public.current_user_id()`.
2. `app/(lister)/inbox/applications.tsx` — the screen (new).
3. `app/(lister)/inbox/_layout.tsx` — bare `<Slot />`; parent Applications/Submissions control deferred to US-058.
4. `app/(lister)/inbox/index.tsx` — `<Redirect href="/(lister)/inbox/applications" />`.
5. `src/types/supabase.ts` — regenerated types (Functions.list_my_applications_as_lister now present).
6. (Deleted) `app/(lister)/inbox.tsx` — was a placeholder using TabPlaceholder; replaced by the directory above.

## Relevant spec sections (read for context)
- `docs/product-plan.md` — US-056 is in the lister-reviews-applications phase.
- `docs/design.md` §3.2 — Inbox is a 4th tab on the lister bar; rows grouped by campaign with SectionHeader; default segment is whichever has higher unreviewed count (ties to Submissions — not in scope for US-056 since the Submissions tab lands in US-058); tapping a row pushes to the campaign-scoped review screen (that's US-057+, so this iteration leaves rows non-interactive).
- `docs/tech-architecture.md` §4.7 — applications schema, RLS policies `applications_lister_read` (select gated through listings.lister_id = caller) and `applications_lister_decide` (update).

## Known context — don't flag as issues
- Realtime publication: `public.applications` already added in US-044's migration `us_044_applications_realtime_publication`. Includes all event types by default.
- Codebase Pattern #117: PostgREST embeds would silently null the creator_profiles/social_links joins because their RLS is fragile — DEFINER RPC + explicit ownership gate is canonical here (like US-043 `list_my_applications`, US-054 `list_my_campaigns`).
- Codebase Pattern #118: `list_my_<thing>` DEFINER skeleton — argumentless, public schema, `set search_path = ''`.
- Codebase Pattern #121: enum→segment mappings as `Record<Enum, Segment>` so exhaustiveness is compile-time enforced.
- Codebase Pattern #122: two-phase probe — DevRolePicker account has no data, so mobile verification is empty-state only; populated case is probed with simulated-JWT SQL against the RPC. 6 rows were returned (3 pending, 2 approved, 1 rejected).
- Codebase Pattern #123: realtime on child-of-join tables — no client filter, rely on RLS; the `applications_lister_read` policy gates the stream.
- SectionHeader is inline (per §6 design spec) because there is only one consumer today. Promote to `src/components/primitives/` when a second screen needs it.
- `onPress` on row cards is intentionally omitted — US-057 wires the review sheet.

## What to look at (high-priority signal)
- The RPC SQL: correctness of joins, status filter, ownership gate, search_path hardening, grant/revoke pattern.
- Realtime subscription lifecycle: cleanup on unmount, re-subscription on userId changes, token retrieval edge cases, ref-mirroring of `load`.
- Segment/bucket typing: the `STATUS_BUCKET` Record excludes withdrawn + cancelled_* via `Exclude` — is this sound? The `bucketFor` helper returns `null` for those three — does any code path pass a row with those statuses? (RPC filters them out, so no, but verify.)
- `formatCount`: thousands/millions formatting — edge cases, zero handling, negative numbers.
- Toast on realtime INSERT: does it fire once per INSERT or could it spam on rapid inserts?
- `onRefresh` vs `load`: RefreshControl spinner hides correctly?
- Accessibility: row cards are non-interactive Views with accessibilityLabel — is that coherent?

## What I want from you
A terse report. Filter strictly: only flag issues where you are genuinely confident they would cause bugs, security problems, or violate the project's documented conventions. Ignore style nits. If there's nothing worth reporting, say so. Report in under 300 words.

content.text:
Reviewing US-056 — `list_my_applications_as_lister` RPC + `applications.tsx`.

Two issues worth flagging:

---

**Critical**

**Realtime toast fires on every INSERT, including the lister's own trigger re-fetches — no deduplication window** (Confidence: 82)

File: `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/app/(lister)/inbox/applications.tsx`, lines 215–222

The INSERT handler immediately calls `loadRef.current()` and `showToast(...)` synchronously in the same tick. If Supabase delivers two INSERT events in rapid succession (e.g. a creator submits two applications to different listings within the same second, which is allowed by the schema), two toasts fire back-to-back with no debounce or dedup guard. This is a real scenario given the inbox aggregates applications across all of the lister's listings. The `load` call itself is cheap but the double-toast is noisy and potentially jarring. The loadRef ref-mirror pattern is correct, but a simple debounce or a `useRef` timestamp guard (`lastToastAt`) would prevent stacking. The `loadRef.current()` re-fetch on every INSERT is fine — it's idempotent — but the `showToast` call has no guard.

Suggested fix: track `lastToastRef = useRef(0)` and only call `showToast` when `Date.now() - lastToastRef.current > 3000`, then update `lastToastRef.current = Date.now()`.

---

**Important**

**`formatCount` returns wrong output for negative numbers** (Confidence: 80)

File: `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/app/(lister)/inbox/applications.tsx`, lines 424–429

`formatCount(-500)` returns `"-500"` (correct), but `formatCount(-1500)` returns `"-1.5K"` (correct by accident — the division and template literal work). However `formatCount(-500_000)` hits the `n < 1_000_000` branch and produces `"-500K"`. The real risk is `follower_count` columns in `creator_profiles` — the DB column is `integer` with no check constraint visible in the migration. If a bad Apify sync writes a negative value, the UI surfaces it silently. This is a minor data-integrity concern rather than a UI crash, but worth a `Math.max(0, n)` clamp or an explicit `if (n < 0) return '—'` guard since follower counts are never legitimately negative.

---

**No issues found in the SQL migration.** The RPC is well-formed: `set search_path = ''` is present, all references are schema-qualified, the ownership gate (`l.lister_id = public.current_user_id()`) is on the `join` table not an application column (so it cannot be bypassed by an orphaned application row), the `social_links` dedup argument via `status <> 'unlinked'` is sound given the partial unique index documented in the comment, and the grant/revoke pattern matches the codebase skeleton.

1	# Ralph Progress Log — Marketify
2	Started: (will be replaced on first run)
3	---
4	
5	## Codebase Patterns
6	*Append-only consolidated learnings. Each future iteration reads this BEFORE doing anything.*
7	
8	- **Authoritative specs are `docs/product-plan.md`, `docs/design.md`, `docs/tech-architecture.md`.** When the story is ambiguous, read the relevant section there. Do not invent product behavior.
9	- **Use Supabase MCP, not the supabase CLI**, for migrations, queries, edge functions, and type generation. Migrations are append-only and named `us_NNN_description`.
10	- **Use mobile-mcp for any UI verification.** Never claim a UI story is done without a screenshot from `mobile_take_screenshot`.
11	- **Use the `feature-dev:code-reviewer` subagent before every commit.** It catches drift across iterations that you can't see in your fresh context.
12	- **Delegate exploration.** If a story would have you read >3 files, spawn the `Explore` subagent first and work from its summary.
13	- **Always regenerate `src/types/supabase.ts`** after any schema migration. Commit the regenerated file in the same commit as the migration.
14	- **Use `bun`, not npm/yarn/pnpm.** It is what's installed locally.
15	- **JWT secret is `MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET`**, separate from Supabase's built-in JWT secret. We sign our own per docs/product-plan.md §2.
16	- **Apify key is `APIFY_KEY`** in `.env`. Read via `process.env.APIFY_KEY` or `Deno.env.get("APIFY_KEY")`. Never paste into source.
17	- **Edge functions live in `supabase/functions/<name>/index.ts`** with shared utils under `supabase/functions/_shared/`.
18	- **Component organization:** primitives in `src/components/primitives/`, screen-specific in `src/screens/<screen>/`, shared composites in `src/components/shared/`.
19	- **No emoji in product UI** — Lucide icons only (per docs/design.md).
20	- **One role per account in v1** — no dual-role logic.
21	- **Run `get_advisors({type:'security'})` after every RLS or schema change.** Address every error-level finding before commit.
22	- **`expo lint` default glob is `['src', 'app', 'components']`.** Empty subdirs with only `.gitkeep` cause eslint to exit 2 ("all files ignored"). Keep at least one lintable `.ts`/`.tsx` file in any of those roots (`src/index.ts` with `export {};` was used for US-001).
23	- **Expo bootstrap workaround:** `create-expo-app .` refuses to run when the target dir has dotfiles/dirs it considers hostile. Bootstrap into a sibling folder (e.g. `.expo-init`), then move the generated files up. Preserves `.claude/`, `.env`, `docs/`, `scripts/`.
24	- **Reanimated 4.x babel plugin is `react-native-worklets/plugin`** (not the legacy `react-native-reanimated/plugin`). It lives in the `react-native-worklets` package — pin it explicitly via `bunx expo install react-native-worklets` so `bun install --production` won't drop it. Included by default in fresh Expo SDK 54 templates.
25	- **Prefer `bunx expo install <pkg>`** over `bun add` for native modules — it pins the SDK-compatible version. Expo's install runs `bun add` under the hood when a `bun.lock` is present.
26	- **App entry is `"main": "expo-router/entry"`** in package.json. The old `index.ts` + `App.tsx` from US-001 were removed. Root layout lives at `app/_layout.tsx`; initial route at `app/index.tsx`.
27	- **Expo Go + SDK 54 + new-arch + `<Stack>` from expo-router throws at runtime**: `Exception in HostFunction: TypeError: expected dynamic type 'boolean', but had type 'string'`. For US-002 the root uses `<Slot />` as the minimal root. US-031 will revisit with a dev-client build (`expo run:ios`) where Stack + Tabs should work.
28	- **iOS simulator boot flow for mobile-mcp verification:** `xcrun simctl boot <name>` → `open -a Simulator` → `bunx expo start --ios` in background → `xcrun simctl openurl <udid> exp://127.0.0.1:8081` if Expo Go doesn't auto-open the project. mobile-mcp's `mobile_open_url` blocks `exp://` schemes by default, so shell out.
29	- **Supabase project:** `marketify` (ref=`[REDACTED_SUPABASE_PROJECT_REF]`, region=`ap-southeast-1`). All MCP DB calls use this `project_id`. Pre-installed extensions: pgcrypto, uuid-ossp, pg_stat_statements, supabase_vault, pg_graphql. pg_cron + citext available on request.
30	- **Install extensions `with schema extensions`** (e.g. `create extension if not exists citext with schema extensions`) to keep advisors clean. `pg_cron` is the exception: Supabase requires it in `pg_catalog`, leave it unqualified.
31	- **Enum canonical list is docs/tech-architecture.md §4.2** (lines 399-427). After generating types, verify `src/types/supabase.ts` Constants block matches it exactly.
32	- **Local migration files live under `supabase/migrations/` with `YYYYMMDDHHMMSS_us_NNN_description.sql`.** Keep filename and MCP migration `name` (no timestamp prefix) consistent. Commit every applied migration — `list_migrations` is the DB side, the file is the source of truth for fresh applies.
33	- **`app_private` schema was created in US-003** via a companion migration (`us_003_app_private_schema`). Revoke is already in place for `anon` and `authenticated`. Triggers / security-definer functions in later stories should target this schema.
34	- **Reference `citext` as `extensions.citext` in every migration.** US-003 installed it with `with schema extensions`; the default `search_path` used by migrations does not include that schema, so an unqualified `citext` fails to resolve.
35	- **Supabase auto-enables RLS on new `public.*` tables** on this project. New table migrations will show up in `get_advisors({type:'security'})` as INFO-level `rls_enabled_no_policy` (not `rls_disabled`). These INFO findings are benign until policies land in US-009 — no commit-blocking action needed.
36	- **`listing_conditions` is a single data-driven table**, not separate pre/post tables. Discriminated by `condition_kind` enum ('pre' | 'post') per docs/tech-architecture.md §4.7 block 8. Pre-condition rows use metrics like `min_followers`; post-condition rows use `post_*` metrics. The story US-005 text named separate tables but the spec wins.
37	- **Circular FK pattern:** `listings.current_version_id` references `listing_versions(id)` but both tables are in the same migration. Create the `listings` table with the column nullable, create `listing_versions`, then `alter table public.listings add constraint ... foreign key ...`. Spec follows this pattern (§4.7 lines 659-661).
38	- **Supabase defaults views to `security_invoker=off` (effectively DEFINER)**; it will fail the `security_definer_view` advisor. Every view we create must be followed with `alter view <name> set (security_invoker = on)` to match the project standard at §4.1 line 375. Applied to `submission_reuse_view` in US-006.
39	- **RLS helper pattern (use for every policy that scopes to the caller):** two SQL `stable` helpers `public.current_user_id()` (returns `nullif(auth.jwt() ->> 'sub', '')::uuid`) and `public.current_user_role()` (returns `nullif(auth.jwt() ->> 'app_role', '')::public.user_role`). Both use `set search_path = ''` and schema-qualify every ref. `nullif` on the empty string makes a missing claim safely return NULL (all `id = current_user_id()` checks fail closed); for `current_user_role()` this is a hardening deviation from the spec's verbatim `(auth.jwt() ->> 'role')::user_role` — intentional, keeps the fn from raising on malformed JWTs. **Note the `app_role` claim (not `role`)** — the bare `role` claim is reserved by PostgREST for the Postgres DB role (always `"authenticated"` on our tokens); see Pattern #JWT-Claims below for why. Policies call these helpers instead of inlining `auth.jwt()` for readability + single-source-of-truth. Ref: `supabase/migrations/20260418020000_us_jwt_app_role_claim.sql` (supersedes the `role`-claim version from `us_009_rls_policies.sql`).
40	- **The `submission_reuse_view` → `submission_reuse_count(uuid)` function swap (US-009 resolution):** the view with `security_invoker=on` would be filtered by the caller's `submissions_lister_read` policy and hide cross-lister reuses (the ReuseBadge's entire purpose). The fix: **drop the view** and replace with a SECURITY DEFINER function `public.submission_reuse_count(p_submission_id uuid) returns integer` that (1) does its own ownership check via join through `submissions → applications → listings.lister_id = current_user_id()`, (2) returns NULL when the caller isn't the listing owner, (3) bypasses RLS to count cross-lister reuses. Revoked from public, granted to authenticated + service_role. Keep this pattern for any other cross-tenant aggregate the UI needs.

## Codebase Patterns
*Append-only consolidated learnings. Each future iteration reads this BEFORE doing anything.*

- **Authoritative specs are `docs/product-plan.md`, `docs/design.md`, `docs/tech-architecture.md`.** When the story is ambiguous, read the relevant section there. Do not invent product behavior.
- **Use Supabase MCP, not the supabase CLI**, for migrations, queries, edge functions, and type generation. Migrations are append-only and named `us_NNN_description`.
- **Use mobile-mcp for any UI verification.** Never claim a UI story is done without a screenshot from `mobile_take_screenshot`.
- **Use the `feature-dev:code-reviewer` subagent before every commit.** It catches drift across iterations that you can't see in your fresh context.
- **Delegate exploration.** If a story would have you read >3 files, spawn the `Explore` subagent first and work from its summary.
- **Always regenerate `src/types/supabase.ts`** after any schema migration. Commit the regenerated file in the same commit as the migration.
- **Use `bun`, not npm/yarn/pnpm.** It is what's installed locally.
- **JWT secret is `MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET`**, separate from Supabase's built-in JWT secret. We sign our own per docs/product-plan.md §2.

- **Edge functions live in `supabase/functions/<name>/index.ts`** with shared utils under `supabase/functions/_shared/`.
- **Component organization:** primitives in `src/components/primitives/`, screen-specific in `src/screens/<screen>/`, shared composites in `src/components/shared/`.
- **No emoji in product UI** — Lucide icons only (per docs/design.md).
- **One role per account in v1** — no dual-role logic.
- **Run `get_advisors({type:'security'})` after every RLS or schema change.** Address every error-level finding before commit.
- **`expo lint` default glob is `['src', 'app', 'components']`.** Empty subdirs with only `.gitkeep` cause eslint to exit 2 ("all files ignored"). Keep at least one lintable `.ts`/`.tsx` file in any of those roots (`src/index.ts` with `export {};` was used for US-001).
- **Expo bootstrap workaround:** `create-expo-app .` refuses to run when the target dir has dotfiles/dirs it considers hostile. Bootstrap into a sibling folder (e.g. `.expo-init`), then move the generated files up. Preserves `.claude/`, `.env`, `docs/`, `scripts/`.
- **Reanimated 4.x babel plugin is `react-native-worklets/plugin`** (not the legacy `react-native-reanimated/plugin`). It lives in the `react-native-worklets` package — pin it explicitly via `bunx expo install react-native-worklets` so `bun install --production` won't drop it. Included by default in fresh Expo SDK 54 templates.
- **Prefer `bunx expo install <pkg>`** over `bun add` for native modules — it pins the SDK-compatible version. Expo's install runs `bun add` under the hood when a `bun.lock` is present.
- **App entry is `"main": "expo-router/entry"`** in package.json. The old `index.ts` + `App.tsx` from US-001 were removed. Root layout lives at `app/_layout.tsx`; initial route at `app/index.tsx`.
- **Expo Go + SDK 54 + new-arch + `<Stack>` from expo-router throws at runtime**: `Exception in HostFunction: TypeError: expected dynamic type 'boolean', but had type 'string'`. For US-002 the root uses `<Slot />` as the minimal root. US-031 will revisit with a dev-client build (`expo run:ios`) where Stack + Tabs should work.
- **iOS simulator boot flow for mobile-mcp verification:** `xcrun simctl boot <name>` → `open -a Simulator` → `bunx expo start --ios` in background → `xcrun simctl openurl <udid> exp://127.0.0.1:8081` if Expo Go doesn't auto-open the project. mobile-mcp's `mobile_open_url` blocks `exp://` schemes by default, so shell out.
- **Supabase project:** `marketify` (ref=`[REDACTED_SUPABASE_PROJECT_REF]`, region=`ap-southeast-1`). All MCP DB calls use this `project_id`. Pre-installed extensions: pgcrypto, uuid-ossp, pg_stat_statements, supabase_vault, pg_graphql. pg_cron + citext available on request.
- **Install extensions `with schema extensions`** (e.g. `create extension if not exists citext with schema extensions`) to keep advisors clean. `pg_cron` is the exception: Supabase requires it in `pg_catalog`, leave it unqualified.
- **Enum canonical list is docs/tech-architecture.md §4.2** (lines 399-427). After generating types, verify `src/types/supabase.ts` Constants block matches it exactly.
- **Local migration files live under `supabase/migrations/` with `YYYYMMDDHHMMSS_us_NNN_description.sql`.** Keep filename and MCP migration `name` (no timestamp prefix) consistent. Commit every applied migration — `list_migrations` is the DB side, the file is the source of truth for fresh applies.
- **`app_private` schema was created in US-003** via a companion migration (`us_003_app_private_schema`). Revoke is already in place for `anon` and `authenticated`. Triggers / security-definer functions in later stories should target this schema.
- **Reference `citext` as `extensions.citext` in every migration.** US-003 installed it with `with schema extensions`; the default `search_path` used by migrations does not include that schema, so an unqualified `citext` fails to resolve.
- **Supabase auto-enables RLS on new `public.*` tables** on this project. New table migrations will show up in `get_advisors({type:'security'})` as INFO-level `rls_enabled_no_policy` (not `rls_disabled`). These INFO findings are benign until policies land in US-009 — no commit-blocking action needed.
- **`listing_conditions` is a single data-driven table**, not separate pre/post tables. Discriminated by `condition_kind` enum ('pre' | 'post') per docs/tech-architecture.md §4.7 block 8. Pre-condition rows use metrics like `min_followers`; post-condition rows use `post_*` metrics. The story US-005 text named separate tables but the spec wins.
- **Circular FK pattern:** `listings.current_version_id` references `listing_versions(id)` but both tables are in the same migration. Create the `listings` table with the column nullable, create `listing_versions`, then `alter table public.listings add constraint ... foreign key ...`. Spec follows this pattern (§4.7 lines 659-661).
- **Supabase defaults views to `security_invoker=off` (effectively DEFINER)**; it will fail the `security_definer_view` advisor. Every view we create must be followed with `alter view <name> set (security_invoker = on)` to match the project standard at §4.1 line 375. Applied to `submission_reuse_view` in US-006.
- **RLS helper pattern (use for every policy that scopes to the caller):** two SQL `stable` helpers `public.current_user_id()` (returns `nullif(auth.jwt() ->> 'sub', '')::uuid`) and `public.current_user_role()` (returns `nullif(auth.jwt() ->> 'app_role', '')::public.user_role`). Policies call these helpers instead of inlining `auth.jwt()` for readability + single-source-of-truth. Ref: `supabase/migrations/20260418020000_us_jwt_app_role_claim.sql` (supersedes the `role`-claim version from `us_009_rls_policies.sql`).
- **The `submission_reuse_view` → `submission_reuse_count(uuid)` function swap (US-009 resolution):** the view with `security_invoker=on` would be filtered by the caller's `submissions_lister_read` policy and hide cross-lister reuses (the ReuseBadge's entire purpose). The fix: **drop the view** and replace with a SECURITY DEFINER function `public.submission_reuse_count(p_submission_id uuid) returns integer` that (1) does its own ownership check via join through `submissions → applications → listings.lister_id = current_user_id()`, (2) returns NULL when the caller isn't the listing owner, (3) bypasses RLS to count cross-lister reuses. Revoked from public, granted to authenticated + service_role. Keep this pattern for any other cross-tenant aggregate the UI needs.

content.content:
140	
141	## 2026-04-18 07:10 — US-050
142	- New screen `app/(lister)/campaigns/new/step-2.tsx` (~280 lines): header "Step 2 of 5 / Who can apply?" + stacked condition cards (each: platform chips TikTok/Instagram, metric chips Followers/Avg views, fixed `≥` operator badge, numeric threshold input, Remove icon button) + "Add condition" secondary CTA + "Next" primary CTA. Pre-conditions are optional — empty list is a valid "accept anyone" listing, Next is always enabled.
143	- Extended `src/screens/campaign-wizard/WizardStore.tsx` with `updateField<K>(key, (prev) => next)` alongside the existing `setField(key, value)`. Step-2's add/remove/update-row callbacks all use `updateField` so the functional updater reads the latest array at React commit time, not at closure-bind time — addresses code-reviewer finding #1 (confidence 90) about rapid-fire taps dropping writes.
144	- Added `maxFontSizeMultiplier={1.3}` to all content `Text` nodes on step-2 per Codebase Pattern #126 (title, subtitle, empty hint, "Rule N" card title, Platform/Metric group labels). UI chrome on the card (the `≥` operator badge, step label) keeps `allowFontScaling={false}`. Addresses code-reviewer finding #2 (confidence 85).
145	- Files changed:
146	  - `app/(lister)/campaigns/new/step-2.tsx` (new)
147	  - `src/screens/campaign-wizard/WizardStore.tsx` (updateField added; setPartial unchanged)
148	  - `scripts/ralph/screenshots/us-050-empty.png` (new)
149	  - `scripts/ralph/screenshots/us-050-two-rows.png` (new)
150	  - `scripts/ralph/prd.json` (US-050 passes:true + notes)
151	  - `scripts/ralph/progress.txt` (this entry + new Codebase Pattern)
152	- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors n/a (UI-only story, no DDL) / mobile-mcp ✓ (iPhone 17 Pro sim iOS 26.3 — Expo Go — dev-signin-lister → FAB → step-1 restored from AsyncStorage draft → Next → step-2 empty-state render → Add condition x2 → Row 1 set to TikTok/Followers/10000, Row 2 set to Instagram/Avg views/5000, both rows persist independently, Add+Next CTAs remain visible + enabled, screenshots captured).
153	- Code-reviewer verdict: issues fixed in this commit. IMPORTANT (confidence 90): stale-closure in row mutation callbacks — fixed by adding `updateField` functional-updater to the store and rewiring all three callbacks. IMPORTANT (confidence 85): content Text nodes missing `maxFontSizeMultiplier={1.3}` per Codebase Pattern #126 — fixed on all six content nodes. IMPORTANT (confidence 82): inline arrow functions in Chip `onPress` defeating `useCallback` memoization — accepted as-is because (a) the reviewer explicitly rated this as "pattern inconsistency and latent perf issue, but not an observable functional bug today", (b) it matches the sibling step-1 pattern, and (c) the per-card array is bounded at 2 platforms + 2 metrics so the cost is negligible. Promoting to a shared primitive would be premature abstraction.
154	- **Learnings for future iterations:**
155	  - **When a context exposes `setField(key, value)`, audit every call site that derives `value` from the current state — those are latent stale-closures.** Symptom: rapid-fire handlers (tap-twice before re-render) silently drop writes. Fix: thread a functional-updater `updateField(key, prev => next)` through the context. Same pattern as React's `setState(fn)` vs `setState(value)`. Promoted to Codebase Patterns.
156	  - **The WizardStore's persistence path (`schedulePersist` inside the functional setDraft) works correctly with `updateField` because `schedulePersist` always sees the freshly-computed `next` object.** No separate persistence fix needed — the 250ms debounce continues to coalesce multiple rapid updates, and the unmount-flush path in WizardStore's cleanup effect still reads from `draftRef.current` which is always the latest. Adding updateField is a pure additive change to the public API.
157	  - **US-044 remains infra-blocked this iteration** — realtime logs still show only the `_messages_` replication slot starting, no `supabase_realtime_replication_slot_v2_83_2` (postgres_changes), and `UnableToConnectToProject` errors continue. Per the established pattern (commits 11f377d, 47b51c3, 4th re-verify attempt), skipped the probe to avoid loop-indefinitely thrash and moved forward to US-050. Next iteration should re-check realtime logs first; if the postgres_changes slot starts cleanly, run `bun run scripts/test/probe-us044-realtime.ts` and close US-044.
158	---
159	
160	## 2026-04-18 02:45 — US-043 verification close-out
161	- JWT drift was resolved upstream by US-036's claim-structure fix (auth.jwt() ->> 'app_role'); probe-jwt-drift.ts now returns 200 on PostgREST. Re-ran end-to-end mobile + HTTP verification to close US-043 cleanly without any code/migration changes.
162	- Mobile-mcp captured 4 empty-state screenshots (us-043-{pending,approved,rejected,cancelled}-empty.png) from the in-app DevRolePicker creator session — confirms segmented control + tap-switching across all 4 segments + each segment's bespoke empty copy renders correctly under iPhone 17 Pro (iOS 26.3) sim. Segment buttons are NOT in the a11y tree (similar to TabBar Pattern #104) — coord-tapped at PENDING(58,138) APPROVED(130,138) REJECTED(222,138) CANCELLED(78,178).
163	- New verification probe `scripts/test/probe-us043-list-apps.ts` (two-phase signup/query): signed up a fresh probe creator via auth-signup-creator → captured a real Marketify JWT → seeded 4 applications for that creator via execute_sql (pending/Acme, approved/Luxe, rejected/Acme, cancelled_listing_edit/Luxe — same partial-unique-friendly distribution as the original us_043_seed_applications migration) → called /rest/v1/rpc/list_my_applications with the JWT → received 4 rows covering all 4 buckets, with non-null lister_handle (@acme_studio) + listing_title resolved on every row. Probe verdict: PASS. Test fixture deleted via execute_sql immediately after.
164	- Files changed:
165	  - `scripts/test/probe-us043-list-apps.ts` (new)
166	  - `scripts/ralph/screenshots/us-043-pending-empty.png` (new)
167	  - `scripts/ralph/screenshots/us-043-approved-empty.png` (new)
168	  - `scripts/ralph/screenshots/us-043-rejected-empty.png` (new)
169	  - `scripts/ralph/screenshots/us-043-cancelled-empty.png` (new)
170	  - `scripts/ralph/prd.json` (US-043 passes:true + updated notes)
171	  - `scripts/ralph/progress.txt` (this entry + new Codebase Pattern)
172	- No app source / migrations / generated types touched in this iteration.
173	- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors ✓ (no DDL changes since US-043 original commit; advisor state unchanged) / mobile-mcp ✓
174	- Code-reviewer verdict: issues fixed in this commit. IMPORTANT (confidence 92): probe's `bucket()` collapsed `withdrawn → cancelled` via implicit else-fallthrough rather than an explicit map mirroring the screen's STATUS_BUCKET; replaced with a typed `Record<Row['status'], Bucket>` so a future 7th enum value trips tsc instead of silently bucketing as 'cancelled'. MINOR (confidence 85): JWT echoed to stdout in signup mode; addressed with an explicit comment documenting the short-lived TTL + immediate fixture-deletion expectation. NIT (confidence 80): `body: '{}'` vs `JSON.stringify({})` skipped — functionally identical, not worth churn. Verification posture (empty-state mobile + populated HTTP probe) explicitly endorsed as a defensible substitute for "screenshot each segment with seeded data" against the US-036/US-038 precedent.
175	- **Learnings for future iterations:**
176	  - The two-phase signup/query probe pattern is the canonical workaround for any screen whose DevRolePicker session can't reach populated rows. Promoted to Codebase Patterns. US-047 (my-submissions tab) will need the same shape — copy this probe's skeleton, swap `list_my_applications` → `list_my_submissions`, and remember to delete the fixture cascade explicitly (the user row is NOT cascaded to social_links/creator_profiles/metric_snapshots — the cleanup statement in this iteration's progress.txt entry is the canonical order).
177	  - The DB-vs-PostgREST verification distinction matters: SQL-level RLS verification (existing US-043 commit, transactional `set local role authenticated` block) proves the function body is correct, but only an HTTP probe under a real JWT proves the PostgREST gateway accepts it AND the function's grant chain to `authenticated` works in production. Both are needed for any RPC-backed screen.
178	  - When a screen's empty copy varies per state (US-043's 4 distinct empty messages per segment), screenshot each empty state — not just one — so the design.md §5.5 empty-copy contract is verified independently of any populated rendering. Cheap-to-take, high diagnostic value.
179	  - DO NOT commit a probe that prints credentials to stdout without a comment block explaining the TTL bound + the operator's responsibility to delete the test fixture. The pattern is acceptable for ephemeral dev probes, dangerous for anything that survives the run. This rule is now in the Codebase Patterns section above (two-phase verification probe pattern).
180	---
181	
182	## 2026-04-18 00:38 — US-043
183	- New screen `app/(creator)/applications.tsx` (~290 lines) replaces the placeholder: `<SafeAreaView>` → `<ScrollView>` → "Applied" display header → inline `SegmentControl` (4 pills: Pending / Approved / Rejected / Cancelled, accessibilityRole='tab' + accessibilityState={selected}) → content area. Content states: loading (two SkeletonCards), error ("Couldn't load applications."), empty-per-segment (bespoke copy), or a list of `ApplicationRowCard`s. Each card: campaign title (listing.title → version snapshot title → "Untitled campaign"), `@lister_handle`, StatusPill, "Applied {formatRelativeTime}" — tap routes to `/(creator)/listing/{listing_id}`. 4-segment UI mapped from the 6-value `application_status` enum via a typed `STATUS_BUCKET: Record<ApplicationStatus, Segment>` (withdrawn + cancelled_listing_edit + cancelled_listing_closed → 'cancelled').
184	- New migration `supabase/migrations/20260418010100_us_043_list_my_applications_rpc.sql`: SECURITY DEFINER SQL function `public.list_my_applications()` returns table of flat joined rows (id, status, created_at, listing_id, listing_title, lister_handle, version_title). Filters by `a.creator_id = public.current_user_id()`. Grants: revoke all from public/anon, grant execute to authenticated. Required because a PostgREST embed `.select('..., lister:users!lister_id(username), version:listing_versions!...(snapshot)')` would silently NULL `lister_handle` for every row (users RLS is self-only) and the version snapshot for non-active listings (listing_versions RLS gates on listing.status='active' OR caller=lister).

Idempotent via `on conflict (id) do nothing`.
186	- Verified via SQL under a simulated creator JWT (`begin; set local role authenticated; set local "request.jwt.claims" = '{"sub":"11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111002","role":"creator"}'; select * from public.list_my_applications();`) — returns all 4 rows with populated listing_title + lister_handle + version_title, confirming the DEFINER bypass works and the ownership check still gates.

## 2026-04-18 07:10 — US-050
- New screen `app/(lister)/campaigns/new/step-2.tsx` (~280 lines): header "Step 2 of 5 / Who can apply?" + stacked condition cards (each: platform chips TikTok/Instagram, metric chips Followers/Avg views, fixed `≥` operator badge, numeric threshold input, Remove icon button) + "Add condition" secondary CTA + "Next" primary CTA. Pre-conditions are optional — empty list is a valid "accept anyone" listing, Next is always enabled.
- Extended `src/screens/campaign-wizard/WizardStore.tsx` with `updateField<K>(key, (prev) => next)` alongside the existing `setField(key, value)`. Step-2's add/remove/update-row callbacks all use `updateField` so the functional updater reads the latest array at React commit time, not at closure-bind time — addresses code-reviewer finding #1 (confidence 90) about rapid-fire taps dropping writes.
- Added `maxFontSizeMultiplier={1.3}` to all content `Text` nodes on step-2 per Codebase Pattern #126 (title, subtitle, empty hint, "Rule N" card title, Platform/Metric group labels). UI chrome on the card (the `≥` operator badge, step label) keeps `allowFontScaling={false}`. Addresses code-reviewer finding #2 (confidence 85).
- Files changed:
  - `app/(lister)/campaigns/new/step-2.tsx` (new)
  - `src/screens/campaign-wizard/WizardStore.tsx` (updateField added; setPartial unchanged)
  - `scripts/ralph/screenshots/us-050-empty.png` (new)
  - `scripts/ralph/screenshots/us-050-two-rows.png` (new)
  - `scripts/ralph/prd.json` (US-050 passes:true + notes)
  - `scripts/ralph/progress.txt` (this entry + new Codebase Pattern)
- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors n/a (UI-only story, no DDL) / mobile-mcp ✓ (iPhone 17 Pro sim iOS 26.3 — Expo Go — dev-signin-lister → FAB → step-1 restored from AsyncStorage draft → Next → step-2 empty-state render → Add condition x2 → Row 1 set to TikTok/Followers/10000, Row 2 set to Instagram/Avg views/5000, both rows persist independently, Add+Next CTAs remain visible + enabled, screenshots captured).
- Code-reviewer verdict: issues fixed in this commit. IMPORTANT (confidence 90): stale-closure in row mutation callbacks — fixed by adding `updateField` functional-updater to the store and rewiring all three callbacks. IMPORTANT (confidence 85): content Text nodes missing `maxFontSizeMultiplier={1.3}` per Codebase Pattern #126 — fixed on all six content nodes. IMPORTANT (confidence 82): inline arrow functions in Chip `onPress` defeating `useCallback` memoization — accepted as-is because (a) the reviewer explicitly rated this as "pattern inconsistency and latent perf issue, but not an observable functional bug today", (b) it matches the sibling step-1 pattern, and (c) the per-card array is bounded at 2 platforms + 2 metrics so the cost is negligible. Promoting to a shared primitive would be premature abstraction.
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - **When a context exposes `setField(key, value)`, audit every call site that derives `value` from the current state — those are latent stale-closures.** Symptom: rapid-fire handlers (tap-twice before re-render) silently drop writes. Fix: thread a functional-updater `updateField(key, prev => next)` through the context. Same pattern as React's `setState(fn)` vs `setState(value)`. Promoted to Codebase Patterns.
  - **The WizardStore's persistence path (`schedulePersist` inside the functional setDraft) works correctly with `updateField` because `schedulePersist` always sees the freshly-computed `next` object.** No separate persistence fix needed — the 250ms debounce continues to coalesce multiple rapid updates, and the unmount-flush path in WizardStore's cleanup effect still reads from `draftRef.current` which is always the latest. Adding updateField is a pure additive change to the public API.
  - **US-044 remains infra-blocked this iteration** — realtime logs still show only the `_messages_` replication slot starting, no `supabase_realtime_replication_slot_v2_83_2` (postgres_changes), and `UnableToConnectToProject` errors continue. Per the established pattern (commits 11f377d, 47b51c3, 4th re-verify attempt), skipped the probe to avoid loop-indefinitely thrash and moved forward to US-050. Next iteration should re-check realtime logs first; if the postgres_changes slot starts cleanly, run `bun run scripts/test/probe-us044-realtime.ts` and close US-044.
