## 2026-04-18 02:45 — US-043 verification close-out
- JWT drift was resolved upstream by US-036's claim-structure fix (auth.jwt() ->> 'app_role'); probe-jwt-drift.ts now returns 200 on PostgREST. Re-ran end-to-end mobile + HTTP verification to close US-043 cleanly without any code/migration changes.
- Mobile-mcp captured 4 empty-state screenshots (us-043-{pending,approved,rejected,cancelled}-empty.png) from the in-app DevRolePicker creator session — confirms segmented control + tap-switching across all 4 segments + each segment's bespoke empty copy renders correctly under iPhone 17 Pro (iOS 26.3) sim. Segment buttons are NOT in the a11y tree (similar to TabBar Pattern #104) — coord-tapped at PENDING(58,138) APPROVED(130,138) REJECTED(222,138) CANCELLED(78,178).
- New verification probe `scripts/test/probe-us043-list-apps.ts` (two-phase signup/query): signed up a fresh probe creator via auth-signup-creator → captured a real Marketify JWT → seeded 4 applications for that creator via execute_sql (pending/Acme, approved/Luxe, rejected/Acme, cancelled_listing_edit/Luxe — same partial-unique-friendly distribution as the original us_043_seed_applications migration) → called /rest/v1/rpc/list_my_applications with the JWT → received 4 rows covering all 4 buckets, with non-null lister_handle (@acme_studio) + listing_title resolved on every row. Probe verdict: PASS. Test fixture deleted via execute_sql immediately after.

- No app source / migrations / generated types touched in this iteration.
- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors ✓ (no DDL changes since US-043 original commit; advisor state unchanged) / mobile-mcp ✓
- Code-reviewer verdict: issues fixed in this commit. IMPORTANT (confidence 92): probe's `bucket()` collapsed `withdrawn → cancelled` via implicit else-fallthrough rather than an explicit map mirroring the screen's STATUS_BUCKET; replaced with a typed `Record<Row['status'], Bucket>` so a future 7th enum value trips tsc instead of silently bucketing as 'cancelled'. MINOR (confidence 85): JWT echoed to stdout in signup mode; addressed with an explicit comment documenting the short-lived TTL + immediate fixture-deletion expectation. NIT (confidence 80): `body: '{}'` vs `JSON.stringify({})` skipped — functionally identical, not worth churn. Verification posture (empty-state mobile + populated HTTP probe) explicitly endorsed as a defensible substitute for "screenshot each segment with seeded data" against the US-036/US-038 precedent.
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - The two-phase signup/query probe pattern is the canonical workaround for any screen whose DevRolePicker session can't reach populated rows. Promoted to Codebase Patterns. US-047 (my-submissions tab) will need the same shape — copy this probe's skeleton, swap `list_my_applications` → `list_my_submissions`, and remember to delete the fixture cascade explicitly (the user row is NOT cascaded to social_links/creator_profiles/metric_snapshots — the cleanup statement in this iteration's progress.txt entry is the canonical order).
  - The DB-vs-PostgREST verification distinction matters: SQL-level RLS verification (existing US-043 commit, transactional `set local role authenticated` block) proves the function body is correct, but only an HTTP probe under a real JWT proves the PostgREST gateway accepts it AND the function's grant chain to `authenticated` works in production. Both are needed for any RPC-backed screen.
  - When a screen's empty copy varies per state (US-043's 4 distinct empty messages per segment), screenshot each empty state — not just one — so the design.md §5.5 empty-copy contract is verified independently of any populated rendering. Cheap-to-take, high diagnostic value.
  - DO NOT commit a probe that prints credentials to stdout without a comment block explaining the TTL bound + the operator's responsibility to delete the test fixture. The pattern is acceptable for ephemeral dev probes, dangerous for anything that survives the run. This rule is now in the Codebase Patterns section above (two-phase verification probe pattern).

content.content:
135	- **Cascade logic lives in the DB trigger, not the client.** `app_private.bump_listing_version` (us_010) is the single source of truth for which listing fields version-bump (`price_cents`, `currency`, `max_submissions` scalars + the ephemeral `version_bump_reason` column for conditions/sample_videos) and which application rows flip to `cancelled_listing_edit` on commit. Any UI that touches this flow is strictly: (1) count pending apps for the warning modal, (2) issue the PATCH, (3) toast. Don't re-declare which fields cascade in JS — read the trigger if unsure. Ref: `supabase/migrations/20260417150000_us_010_cascade_trigger.sql` + US-055 edit screen.
136	- **Ownership-transitive counts work via direct PostgREST under existing RLS — no RPC wrapper needed.** When the lister needs to count rows on a related table that is RLS-scoped through a transitive ownership path (e.g. `applications` → `listings.lister_id = current_user_id()` via `applications_lister_read`), use `supabase.from('<table>').select('id', {head:true, count:'exact'}).eq(...)` directly. The policy already enforces ownership. No `SECURITY DEFINER` function needed unless you're crossing tenants (see the `submission_reuse_count` pattern above for the cross-tenant case). Ref: pending-app count in `app/(lister)/campaigns/[id]/edit.tsx` `onSave` (US-055).
137	- **When seeding test listings manually, insert the v1 `listing_versions` row in the same statement.** The app's write paths (create-listing edge function) create a v1 `listing_versions` record alongside the `listings` insert, so `applications.listing_version_id` has a non-null FK target. A raw `INSERT INTO listings ...` via execute_sql bypasses this, and subsequent `INSERT INTO applications ...` fails with `null value in column "listing_version_id"`. Cleanup order matters too: delete `applications` first, then `listings` (which FKs to `current_version_id`), then `listing_versions`. Used for US-055 cascade e2e probe.
138

input.new_string:
- **When seeding test listings manually, insert the v1 `listing_versions` row in the same statement.** The app's write paths (create-listing edge function) create a v1 `listing_versions` record alongside the `listings` insert, so `applications.listing_version_id` has a non-null FK target. A raw `INSERT INTO listings ...` via execute_sql bypasses this, and subsequent `INSERT INTO applications ...` fails with `null value in column "listing_version_id"`. Cleanup order matters too: delete `applications` first, then `listings` (which FKs to `current_version_id`), then `listing_versions`. Used for US-055 cascade e2e probe.
- **Toast dedup on realtime broadcast handlers.** When a postgres_changes handler calls `showToast`, guard with a `useRef<number>(0)` + cooldown window (3s works well) so two INSERTs arriving back-to-back (e.g. a creator applies to two listings within the same second, or two creators race) don't stack banners. The re-fetch itself stays idempotent — only the user-facing banner is debounced. Re-subscription does NOT need to happen on the ref change; the handler reads `lastToastAtRef.current` at call time. Promote this to every realtime handler (US-044 creator, US-056 lister inbox, US-058 lister submissions, US-047 creator submissions). Ref: `app/(lister)/inbox/applications.tsx` (US-056).
- **Compact thousands formatter for unbounded integer metrics (follower_count, avg_views).** Inline `formatCount(n: number | null)`: null → '—', negative → '—' (defensive — follower_count has no DB check constraint, a buggy Apify sync could write <0), <1_000 → `toLocaleString('en-US')`, <1_000_000 → `${(n/1000).toFixed(n<10_000 ? 1 : 0)}K`, else → `${(n/1_000_000).toFixed(n<10_000_000 ? 1 : 0)}M`. Promote to `src/lib/format.ts` when a 2nd screen uses it. Ref: `app/(lister)/inbox/applications.tsx`.
- **SectionHeader stays inline until a 2nd consumer lands.** `docs/design.md` §6 specs the `══════ Label ══════` component with `title, action?`. US-056 is the first consumer, so it ships as a local function component (flex row: 2-px ink rule · micro text · 2-px ink rule). Don't promote to `src/components/primitives/` preemptively; the second screen (likely US-058 submissions) will reveal whether the `action?` slot is needed and the promotion becomes trivial at that point. Ref: `app/(lister)/inbox/applications.tsx` `SectionHeader`.
- **Lister-side RPC pattern parallels creator-side.** `list_my_applications_as_lister()` mirrors US-043's `list_my_applications()`: argumentless, SECURITY DEFINER, `set search_path = ''`, filters by `listings.lister_id = public.current_user_id()` + a status whitelist, returns a flat joined row shape (application + listing + creator username + both platform handles + creator_profiles metrics). The status whitelist (pending,approved,rejected) intentionally excludes withdrawn + cancelled_listing_* because those are creator-initiated or cascade-driven cancellations that don't require lister review — they surface on the campaign-scoped detail screen (US-057+), not the inbox. When US-058 lands the submissions variant, use the same skeleton: `list_my_submissions_as_lister()` filtering on `submissions.status in (...)` and joining through `applications.listing_id → listings.lister_id = current_user_id()`. Ref: `supabase/migrations/20260418130000_us_056_list_my_applications_as_lister_rpc.sql`.

---

## 2026-04-18 13:30 — US-056
- New migration `supabase/migrations/20260418130000_us_056_list_my_applications_as_lister_rpc.sql`: SECURITY DEFINER SQL `public.list_my_applications_as_lister()` returns one flat row per application — id/status/created_at/cover_note/listing_id/listing_title/creator_user_id/creator_username/tiktok_handle/tiktok_follower_count/instagram_handle/instagram_follower_count/instagram_avg_views_last_10. Joins `applications a → listings l` (inner; `where l.lister_id = public.current_user_id()`) + left joins to `users`, `creator_profiles`, and two `social_links` filtered `status <> 'unlinked'` (the partial unique index `social_links_active_uniq (user_id, platform) WHERE status <> 'unlinked'` guarantees the left join cannot duplicate). Status filter `in (pending,approved,rejected)` excludes withdrawn + cancelled_listing_* (out-of-scope for review inbox). `set search_path = ''`, schema-qualified throughout, revoke from public/anon, grant execute to authenticated.
- Route refactor: deleted placeholder `app/(lister)/inbox.tsx` (`TabPlaceholder`-based) → new `app/(lister)/inbox/` directory with `_layout.tsx` (bare `<Slot />`; US-058 lands the Applications/Submissions parent segmented control here), `index.tsx` (`<Redirect href="/(lister)/inbox/applications" />`), and `applications.tsx` (the screen). TabBar `/(lister)/inbox` navigation lands on Applications by default.
- New screen `app/(lister)/inbox/applications.tsx` (~420 lines): header "Inbox" + inline 3-segment control (Pending/Approved/Rejected with per-segment count badges). Per-row card shows creator `@username` (display text) + StatusPill on the top row, then two `MetricLine` rows (TikTok + Instagram) with fixed-width platform label + mono-font `@handle` + compact-formatted follower count (and `· X avg views` on IG), then a 2-line clamped pitch preview from `cover_note` (omitted when null), then "Applied {relative}". Cards grouped by campaign with inline `SectionHeader` (`══════ Title ══════` visual per design.md §6). Realtime INSERT subscription on `public.applications` with NO client-side filter — applications has no `lister_id` column, the `applications_lister_read` RLS policy is the security boundary (Codebase Pattern #123); handler re-runs the RPC via `loadRef.current()` (ref-mirror pattern; Pattern #82/#106) because the raw INSERT payload lacks joined creator metrics, and fires an info toast "New application received" guarded by a 3s `lastToastAtRef` cooldown so back-to-back inserts don't stack banners. No per-row `onPress` — US-057 wires the review sheet.
